2249015858.Title: MenuItem mnemonics key binding conflict
2249015858.Author: jkim7x
2249015858.Body:
It is not possible to select "Export Image" using mnemonics to navigate the menu items.
It has the same key binding, "E,' as the top level key binding for "Edit".
If a user attempts to press "E" in the window below, the focus will shift to the "Edit" sub-level menu items instead of executing "Export Image."

![image](https://github.com/prmr/JetUML/assets/113862811/76958435-26ab-4704-9279-42999a4792f2)
2249015858.PlainText:
MenuItem mnemonics key binding conflict

It is not possible to select "Export Image" using mnemonics to navigate the menu items.
It has the same key binding, "E,' as the top level key binding for "Edit".
If a user attempts to press "E" in the window below, the focus will shift to the "Edit" sub-level menu items instead of executing "Export Image."

Comments.Body: Additionally, "Exit," does not have a mnemonic key binding because mnemonics are case sensitive and it should be set to the lowercase "x"
Comments.Author: jkim7x

2281341408.Title: Run geometry tests only on windows
2281341408.Author: prmr
2281341408.Body: The geometry tests ensure that the diagram renders properly. For this purpose it uses pixel coordinates. Because of system fonts and other environment configuration details, these coordinates vary from OS to OS. The current solution is to add values for all 3 main OSes, which is extremely cumbersome and does not provide much value because the underlying geometry calculations are OS-independent. Instead, we will run the tests only on Windows.
2281341408.PlainText:
Run geometry tests only on windows

The geometry tests ensure that the diagram renders properly. For this purpose it uses pixel coordinates. Because of system fonts and other environment configuration details, these coordinates vary from OS to OS. The current solution is to add values for all 3 main OSes, which is extremely cumbersome and does not provide much value because the underlying geometry calculations are OS-independent. Instead, we will run the tests only on Windows.

2288442629.Title: Connection point calculation error in State Transition Diagram
2288442629.Author: prmr
2288442629.Body:
**Description**
When rendering two transition edges between two state nodes, if one of the nodes is in a very specific position in relation to the other node, the connection point for one of the two edges will be located at an incorrect position within the node. This can be observed for the bottom two corners.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Create new State diagram .
2. Create two state nodes, join them by two edges in the same direction.
3. Carefully rotate one node around the other. The misplaced connection point can be observed by the edge flashing when the node is in the precise problematic position.

**Screenshots**
![image](https://github.com/prmr/JetUML/assets/6033492/fc689e71-f9ac-42bc-8a6d-6c1570314b2a)

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 3.7
 
**Additional information**
Probably an issue in `StateTransitionEdgeRenderer`
2288442629.PlainText:
Connection point calculation error in State Transition Diagram

Description
When rendering two transition edges between two state nodes, if one of the nodes is in a very specific position in relation to the other node, the connection point for one of the two edges will be located at an incorrect position within the node. This can be observed for the bottom two corners.
Steps to reproduce
Create new State diagram .
Create two state nodes, join them by two edges in the same direction.
Carefully rotate one node around the other. The misplaced connection point can be observed by the edge flashing when the node is in the precise problematic position.
Screenshots
Configuration
JetUML Release: 3.7
Additional information
Probably an issue in StateTransitionEdgeRenderer

Comments.Body:
It appears that the incorrect position can occur at either connection point of the edge, but always in the north-west and north-east directions:

![image](https://github.com/prmr/JetUML/assets/113862811/126e1b99-e586-4bcc-830c-8b09744f83fc)
Comments.Author: jkim7x

2325150743.Title: Tooltips and menu options are flickering, menu is unusable on Arch Linux, .jar
2325150743.Author: faervan
2325150743.Body:
**Description**
Tooltips and menu options are flickering, menu is unusable as shown in the video

https://github.com/prmr/JetUML/assets/124069539/a3a9622e-9023-4589-9319-d0271cd22ab0

**Steps to reproduce**
1. und 2.
```
sudo pacman -S jdk-openjdk
paru -S java17-openjfx
```
3. Download JetUML-3.7.jar
4. 
```
java --module-path "/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjfx/lib/" --add-modules=javafx.controls,javafx.swing,java.desktop,java.prefs -jar /home/$USERNAME/Downloads/JetUML-3.7.jar
```
6. Notice flickering.

**Screenshots**
\[If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem. Remove this section if not applicable.\]

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: JetUML-3.7.jar
 - OS + version: Arch Linux (up to date)
 - Java version: openjdk 22 2024-03-19
- JavaFX version: java-17-openjfx
 
**Additional information**
How I start JetUML:
```
java --module-path "/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjfx/lib/" --add-modules=javafx.controls,javafx.swing,java.desktop,java.prefs -jar /home/stk/Downloads/JetUML-3.7.jar
```

When I tried to install JetUML using the Arch User Repository, it won't work because it doesn't find my version of JavaFX.
```
==> Starting package()...
cp: cannot stat '/usr/lib/jvm/java-18-openjdk/lib/javafx*': No such file or directory
==> ERROR: A failure occurred in package().
    Aborting...
error: failed to build 'jetuml-3.4-0': 
error: packages failed to build: jetuml-3.4-0
```

2351086266.Title: Unnecessary Scroll Bars in Text Areas
2351086266.Author: jkim7x
2351086266.Body:
**Description**
Scrollbars appear in the text areas of the property window, even though they are not needed.
This seems to be an issue with higher resolution monitors and monitors with a higher scale preference in their display settings.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Set the 'scale' in your system display settings to >= 125%
2. Create new 'Class' diagram 
3. Create a Class node
4. Double click on the node to bring up the Properties window
6. See the scroll bars on the empty text areas

**Screenshots**
![properties](https://github.com/prmr/JetUML/assets/113862811/dddc3c6a-43ee-40b8-8dab-e17116e5ba73)

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 3.7
 - OS + version: Windows 11
 - Java 17
2351086266.PlainText:
Unnecessary Scroll Bars in Text Areas

Description
Scrollbars appear in the text areas of the property window, even though they are not needed.
This seems to be an issue with higher resolution monitors and monitors with a higher scale preference in their display settings.
Steps to reproduce
Set the 'scale' in your system display settings to >= 125%
Create new 'Class' diagram
Create a Class node
Double click on the node to bring up the Properties window
See the scroll bars on the empty text areas
Screenshots
Configuration
JetUML Release: 3.7
OS + version: Windows 11
Java 17

2878871123.Title: OpenBottom no longer visible
2878871123.Author: prmr
2878871123.Body:
**Description**
The "Open Bottom" feature, which allows to flag call nodes in sequence diagrams as incomplete by setting the property to true, no longer render properly.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Open a new sequence diagram
2. Create two implicit parameter nodes and a call between them
3. Double-click on one of the two call nodes.
4. Check "Open Bottom"

The open bottom does not show.

**Screenshots**

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/36a22308-477c-487c-9bcc-2fc1b863dd73)

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 3.8
 - OS + version: All
 - Java version: All

3338434671.Title: Add Maven Build Configuration
3338434671.Author: prmr
3338434671.Body:
Add support for building the project with Maven as an alternative to default building with Eclipse. 

* [ ] The project should remain compilable and runnable in Eclipse with no additional configuration.
* [ ] The build configuration (`pom.xml`) should respect the current directory structure.
* [ ] The build configuration should include options for packaging JetUML as a thin or fat jar, or as a native application installer.

We can also use this opportunity to migrate to Java 25 when it's out.
3338434671.PlainText:
Add Maven Build Configuration

Add support for building the project with Maven as an alternative to default building with Eclipse.
[ ] The project should remain compilable and runnable in Eclipse with no additional configuration.
[ ] The build configuration (pom.xml) should respect the current directory structure.
[ ] The build configuration should include options for packaging JetUML as a thin or fat jar, or as a native application installer.
We can also use this opportunity to migrate to Java 25 when it's out.

53755301.Title: Create a Welcome Panel
53755301.Author: prmr
53755301.Body: The welcome panel should appear whenever there are no diagram, and should support creating new diagrams or opening recent files. Probably a good idea to add this after we switch over to the tabbed panel.
53755301.PlainText:
Create a Welcome Panel

The welcome panel should appear whenever there are no diagram, and should support creating new diagrams or opening recent files. Probably a good idea to add this after we switch over to the tabbed panel.

Comments.Body: I already unhooked the about dialog from the launch sequence.
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: Hey guys, I just added a very rough outline of a WelcomeTab to JetUML. It will need some polishing (mostly just graphical stuff and some way to link in the functionality desired) though. For now, please check it out and let me know if you notice any buggy behavior. For now, it should be rendered on launch, or whenever the last open tab is closed. Also, the Welcome Tab cannot be closed manually when it is open, opening a new Tab is the only way to close it.
Comments.Author: JoelChev

Comments.Body:
@prmr I have pushed a semi-finished version of the WelcomeTab. This version now has the icons and the functionality required of a WelcomeTab. The only issue however is that it isn't pretty (I'm going to need to ask you how to work with the Nimbus theme a bit to make it look better). For now, please play around with the commit and let me know what you think of the WelcomeTab!

Note: This commit is separate from the X Close button I just added to tabs in branch #21. I can integrate that functionality into this commit easily (I was thinking that the Welcome Tab won't have a close button however as it shouldn't be a closeable tab).
Comments.Author: JoelChev

Comments.Body: This seems to work too. It opens and closes properly at the correct time. I agree that the Welcome Tab should not have a close button though. How hard would that be to implement?
Comments.Author: EJBQ

Comments.Body: There are still issues here with trying to add a background to the WelcomeTab with the Nimbus theme enabled. I will show what I mean during our next meeting, but I will need some feedback before I close this issue.
Comments.Author: JoelChev

NPE when loading previous file with unsaved changes

Steps to reproduce:
Create a save a file call it A.violet
Restart the application
Create a new diagram
File -> Recent Files -> a.violet

Comments.Body: This is occurring in the "open" method. It checks each open frame to see if the name of that frame matches the file to open, but doesn't check if the frame name is null, which it is for new diagrams.
Comments.Author: EJBQ

Comments.Body: Fixed by commit c8f06032abbe0e26eb94c7a9dd4c15b54a2a58f2
Comments.Author: EJBQ

Comments.Body: @prmr I have been looking at this problem a bit and I think I may need to implement a means of converting a SelectionList to a Graph. Do you think this will be necessary, or would there be an easier way to copy and paste the contents of a GraphPanel? I'm trying to work from the copy+paste feature of Violet 0.23 right now as a template, but their method is based on a Graph being the object created on a mouse-drag selection, not GraphElements being added to a SelectionList. Any tips or pointers would be appreciated.
Comments.Author: JoelChev

Comments.Body: There's only one graph per panel, so you would want to go through the selection list and add the elements in the selection to the existing graph (and remove them from the original one in the case of cut). They are cloneable so that part should not be too hard. However, the more interesting bit here is to carefully specify the behavior of cut and paste. For instance, if should only work for nodes, but copying multiple nodes should include the edges between them, etc.
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: Actually the cut part should just work because `GraphPanel.removeSelected()` does the right thing. However, implementing the pasting behavior will require a more systematic study. Don't base yourself on the 0.21 behavior because it's highly unsatisfactory. For example, if you cut an edge and paste it, a node appears!
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: Final comment for now: why not start a wiki page with the specification of the Copy-and-Paste Behavior?
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: @prmr I just wrote a wiki page describing the behaviour as I see it. If you think I am missing anything or if you want anything else added, please feel free to edit it. I will think about this problem some more, but I think some major redesign work may be necessary for the pasting part of the operation (I don't believe copying will be too difficult to implement).
Comments.Author: JoelChev

Comments.Body: I'm not sure we need a big redesign. IMO inheriting `Clipboard` from `Graph` as in 0.23 is overkill for our needs. I've just committed some code to branch 0012 that sketches a solution. Look at class `Clipboard` and methods `cut`, `copy`, and `paste` in `EditorFrame`. Implementing these, and the required services in `GraphPanel`, should get you closer to something quite workable.
Comments.Author: prmr
JoelChev

Comments.Body: @prmr I have now got copy+paste working on all diagram types, except for sequence type diagrams. For whatever reason, when I try to copy and paste those types of diagrams, I get really buggy behavior (I don't know why). I think there is something going on with the setting of children/parent nodes that I'm not seeing, but I will keep trying a few more things to get it working. I'm going to try to get a working version of cut going, then I will commit what I have so far.

Comments.Body: Good to know, I'll look at the code for this diagram.

Comments.Body:
Alright, I just committed what I have been able to do with cutting, copying and pasting. @prmr There are still bugs with pasting onto Sequence Diagrams, but everything else seems to be behaving normally. @EJBQ, I have not implemented any functionality for "undoing" or "redoing" a cut, copy or paste action. You will have to figure out a way to implement this, but I am totally open to you modifying my design in any way if you need to for your part of Milestone 2. If anything I have done is unclear, please let me know and I will try to explain what I did here.

I had to modify a few other things in JetUML too, including how edges are selected (now if you drag over an edge in "Select-Mode" with the "Click-Down-Lasso" and both its endpoints have been selected, it is automatically selected). I also updated how children Arraylists are handled in the AbstractNode class during the clone method(before it was giving me IndexOutOfBounds errors when I tried to clone the children of nodes). Anyways, if either of you can please check out my commit and make sure it works properly, I would appreciate it. Thanks!

@prmr Yeah, I will look into the object diagram problem a bit. Another bug I found involved Note Nodes on Class Diagrams. Steps to reproduce:

2. Add a Note Connector with the start end being the Note Node and the End Node being either white space or another Node
3. Notice that the edge either ends in white space or does not connect to the End Node desired.

Interestingly, if you try to start a Note Edge in a non-Note Node, it behaves properly. I'm not sure what is going on her either, but I can try to look into it as well.

If you guys notice any other mistakes or errors, please let me know. Thanks!
Comments.Author: JoelChev

Comments.Body:
# Parent-Child Relation in Sequence Diagrams

(this comment is also relevant to @EJBQ 's work)
It seems a lot of the friction you are experiencing comes from the fact that in sequence diagrams the parent-child relation is defined to map to the parent-child node in the corresponding call graph. This is not actually what we really need for a diagram editor. Instead, in all diagrams the parent-child node relation should be defined as "child node is semantically a component of the parent) (e.g., field nodes in the object diagram). 
I started a wiki page to document the parent-child relations
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body:
@prmr @EJBQ I forgot to tag my recent commits with a #12 (my bad, I will do this again from now on). In my recent two commits, I have fixed two major bugs:
1. The duplication/buggy behavior of Field Nodes that lay inside ObjectNodes when they are cloned. Now the copying/cutting and pasting behaves as it should for this diagram.
2. The weird bug that prevented diagrams from being translated if an edge was in the SelectionList. I had to modify the SelectionList class to make this work properly, essentially, it needed a getLastNode() method not a getLastSelected() method (as an Edge can be the last element in the SelectionList). Take a look at my additions to the GraphPanel and SelectionList classes if this seems confusing.

Anyways, I encourage you guys to check out my latest commit to this branch and play around with it and make sure both issues have been fixed. I will be working on fixing the Sequence Diagram bugs between now and our next meeting. Thanks!
Comments.Author: JoelChev

Comments.Body: I have taken a look into the Sequence Diagram issue a bit, and it is still giving me issues. I think we need to decide as a group if we want to redesign this or not. As you mentioned @prmr, CallNodes can have CallNode children (which to me seems like an odd way to organize things). I would much rather see CallNodes by default be children of ImplicitParameterNodes (unless they are created by a self-call). I don't know what you guys think about this, but I am open to discussing this at our next meeting in further detail.
Comments.Author: JoelChev

Comments.Body: While testing I found a new issue with pasting and the undo system, as well as an issue with double pasting. I'm delaying the release by a day while I fix this.
Comments.Author: EJBQ

Comments.Body: The undoing issue is solved, just waiting on @JoelChev to look at double pasting.
Comments.Author: EJBQ

Comments.Body: @EJBQ @prmr I believe I have solved the double pasting bug, but it involved me modifying the addNode() method of the Graph class. If either of you can please check out #13's latest commit and play around with double pasting nodes with edges (so copy/cut nodes with edges between them, then paste them two or more times in a row), I would appreciate it. Thanks!
Comments.Author: JoelChev

Comments.Body: I'm still working on this. The foundation seems solid, based on the enhanced Violet source with commands and an UndoManager. Right now movement seems like the hardest part, since it's done in many pieces in a different section, harder to do under Demeter's Rule.
Comments.Author: EJBQ

Comments.Body: There are issues with the method split between GraphPanel, GraphFrame, and Graph. I need to use Graph for adding nodes, which requires a reference there, and removing stays inside the Graph too, so I can't track every deleted Edge by staying only in GraphPanel.
Comments.Author: EJBQ

Comments.Body: Also, to update the graph after undoing you need to move the window a bit. It doesn't seem to check whether it should refresh on every frame, but rather when it is called to repaint. I'm working on this still.
Comments.Author: EJBQ

Comments.Body: So I'm hitting the issue that adding nodes and edges happens in the "Graph" class, whereas moving nodes and changing properties happens in the "GraphPanel" class. This means it's hard for me to create the Commands so that I can pass just one or the other. The issue is that calling the method in the Graph (where adding nodes and edges is) has no way to refer to its parent GraphPanel at the moment. It seems less clean to just add a reference to the parent though. And if I don't pass a reference to GraphPanel, then I can't repaint the scene after my action and it remains still until you change something else. @prmr and @JoelChev , do you think I should just add a reference to GraphPanel for now or refactor in some better way?
Comments.Author: EJBQ

Comments.Body: Since there's only one `Graph` per `GraphPanel`, can't you just pass in the `GraphPanel` to your commands and write delegates in `GraphPanel` that forward necessary requests (add, remove) to the graph therein?
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: Yeah, that's probably easier than what I'm doing. I'm going to have to override the MouseAdapter constructor to pass the GraphPanel, but that's much cleaner still.
Comments.Author: EJBQ

Comments.Body: Most things work properly and cleanly now. There's a null pointer issue with properties I will fix tomorrow and children act oddly for sequence diagrams because of how edges delete children, but I have identified these issues.
Comments.Author: EJBQ

Comments.Body: So the issue I'm having is that the "MultiLineString.clone().equals(original) = true" property is not upheld, so that clones do not equal their original, which is a pretty major issue. So my question right now is whether to do the invasive thing that Violet 2.3 did, which is add a PropertyChangeListener to the Property Sheet and the editors therein, or to attempt to change the MultiLineString class in some way. This should not be an issue with classes that preserve the .clone() property correctly. @prmr and @JoelChev, what do you think? As it is right now I've been making sure that everything else works, but that's not really worth the time if I switch to the PropertyChangeEvent.
Comments.Author: EJBQ

Comments.Body: At this point I think everything is properly undoable and redoable. There is an issue with movement of nodes after placement I'm still looking at and another with moving onto another node, but everything does work at this point as far as I can tell. Obviously some testing would be nice, @prmr and @JoelChev.
Comments.Author: EJBQ

Comments.Body: I believe I have fixed the double pasting bug in the latest commit, but I want to make sure I haven't introduced any new bugs in the process. If this commit could be looked at, I would appreciate it!
Comments.Author: JoelChev

53967244.Title: Order properties in the PropertyEditor
53967244.Author: prmr
53967244.Body: Since the refactoring of the graph framework, properties appear in a strange order in the properties editor. This is because the properties editor is sensitive to the arbitrary order in which properties are discovered by the JavaBeans framework, and the fact that the priority property is not used by the comparator in the constructor of `PropertySheet`
53967244.PlainText:
Order properties in the PropertyEditor

Since the refactoring of the graph framework, properties appear in a strange order in the properties editor. This is because the properties editor is sensitive to the arbitrary order in which properties are discovered by the JavaBeans framework, and the fact that the priority property is not used by the comparator in the constructor of PropertySheet

Comments.Body: Actually the priority information is specified in the relectively-loaded `*BeanInfo` classes, which must be found in the same package. I just moved them over and cleaned them up.
Comments.Author: prmr

53967276.Title: Add a diagram type fragment to file extensions
53967276.Author: prmr

Comments.Body:
This issue used to be titled "Move to the Violet >0.16 serialization format" but after a bit of research it seems like the 0.16 and 0.21 formats are the same (JavaBean-enabled), the only difference being that in 0.21 the files get an additional extension fragment to indicate the diagram type. 

However, one of the big drawbacks of the JB serialization framework is that simply renaming the packages breaks backward compatibility. Writing a command-line converter should be simple enough though. See Issue #17.

@EJBQ  @prmr The TabbedView is starting to come along! I invite you both to check it out and play with it a bit. I have yet to look into adding a splash screen (so on launch there is nothing on display, you have to add a tab to start interacting with JetUML). If there are any important features that I have not ported over yet, please let me know and I will look into them.

Some important changes:

For now, I am treating tabs as JInternalFrames (so either GrameFrames or an eventual WelcomeFrame splash tab as we discussed last week). If either of you feel that I should change this to a different Swing component (JPanel or something), please let me know and I can change this quickly. Thanks!

56129369.Title: Make it possible to remove  node from a list of selected graph elements
56129369.Author: prmr
56129369.Body: Using the control key it's possible to add to a list of selected elements. It should be possible to remove selected elements in the same way. In Violet 0.21 that is only possible for nodes so we'll replicate this behavior for now but ultimately this should be more consistent across nodes and edges. This should be based on a cleanup of the mouse handling in `GraphPanel`, which is a bit unprincipled right now.
56129369.PlainText:
Make it possible to remove  node from a list of selected graph elements

Using the control key it's possible to add to a list of selected elements. It should be possible to remove selected elements in the same way. In Violet 0.21 that is only possible for nodes so we'll replicate this behavior for now but ultimately this should be more consistent across nodes and edges. This should be based on a cleanup of the mouse handling in GraphPanel, which is a bit unprincipled right now.

Events.Author: prmr
Events.RenamedFrom: Make it possible to remove from a list of selected graph elements
Events.RenamedTo: Make it possible to remove  node from a list of selected graph elements

56615684.Title: Improved grabber look and feel
56615684.Author: prmr

56615684.PlainText:
Improved grabber look and feel

Modernize the look and feel of the grabber, so it looks prettier.

56911853.Title: In Object Diagrams, make it impossible to add fields nodes that are not a child of an object node
56911853.Author: prmr
56911853.Body: This behavior is already implemented for CallNodes in the sequence diagams.
56911853.PlainText:
In Object Diagrams, make it impossible to add fields nodes that are not a child of an object node

This behavior is already implemented for CallNodes in the sequence diagams.

Comments.Body:
@prmr I have addressed the bug; it is now impossible to add Field Nodes unless they are added into Object Nodes. I have noticed a persistent bug however (it was present even before the update and I have tried to solve it but to no avail).

Steps to reproduce:
1. Use the lasso to select an Object Node with a Field Node inside it.
2. Try to move the selection around. The Field Node becomes temporarily "unhooked" from the Object Node and wiggles around in space while you move. It snaps back into place on release of the mouse.

I have tried looking into Violet 0.23 and into the behaviour of Call Nodes+ImplicitParameter Nodes to find an idea of why the above bug is ocurring, but I can't figure out what the issue is.
Comments.Author: JoelChev
prmr

Comments.Body: @prmr, Alright, I started issue #42 to address the translating bug, which I will tackle today. Can you or @EJBQ please check out this branch and ensure that adding Field Nodes is behaving as desired? If so, I think we can close this issue and I can merge this branch into master. Thanks!
Comments.Author: JoelChev

Comments.Body: It looks to me like it's doing the right thing.
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: Alright, I just merged this into the master branch and pushed it. I will close this now.
Comments.Author: JoelChev

Comments.Body: It seems to work fine for me as well. The only thing I noticed, which doesn't seem to be caused by this change, is that it's not possible to move a Field Node from one Object to another. Is that a feature we want available?
Comments.Author: EJBQ

Comments.Body: I don't think so, at least in the short term (I don't remember ever wanting this feature).
Comments.Author: prmr

56912012.Title: Clean up the persistence code
56912012.Author: prmr
56912012.Body:
The persistence support in `EditorFrame` can be improved:
- The behavior is in static methods that could be moved to a specialized and testable class.
- There is some old workaround code for a JDK bug that has been fixed. We might be able to use the more straightforward strategy.
56912012.PlainText:
Clean up the persistence code

The persistence support in EditorFrame can be improved:
The behavior is in static methods that could be moved to a specialized and testable class.
There is some old workaround code for a JDK bug that has been fixed. We might be able to use the more straightforward strategy.

In Object Diagrams, select call node and point in an object node or other call node, and a call node (erroneously) gets added as a children. This should not be possible.

57063730.Title: Empty Property Editor
57063730.Author: prmr
57063730.Body: In the framework, it is possible to bring up a properties editor even for an object that has no properties. For example, an note edge. This should not happen: if the object has no property, the behavior should default to do nothing.
57063730.PlainText:
Empty Property Editor

In the framework, it is possible to bring up a properties editor even for an object that has no properties. For example, an note edge. This should not happen: if the object has no property, the behavior should default to do nothing.

Comments.Body: I just commited a fix for this; please check it out when you get the chance. I can modify this if necessary. I wasn't paying attention however and Issue0044 (Branch #44) was accidentally created as a new branch from Issue0047+0054  (Bracnh #47 and #54), instead of from the master branch. Consequently the state diagram fixes are in Issue0044 as well. I will be more careful going forward, but I figure it shouldn't be a big deal here as all these issues are not very large in scope. Thanks!
Comments.Author: JoelChev

57065337.Title: File path gets lost during file save
57065337.Author: prmr
57065337.Body:
Steps to reproduce:
1. Create a diagram, save as `testdata/Mydiagram...`
2. Close it
3. Open the diagram with Recent Files
4. Save it using Save

It gets saved in the root directory, not in `testdata`, clearly the path part of the file name has been lost.
57065337.PlainText:
File path gets lost during file save

Steps to reproduce:
Create a diagram, save as testdata/Mydiagram...
Close it
Open the diagram with Recent Files
Save it using Save
It gets saved in the root directory, not in testdata, clearly the path part of the file name has been lost.

Comments.Body: The name of the file is saved as a string in `EditorFrame`, which makes transformations awkward. I will convert this to a `File` so the long or short name can be used as necessary.
Comments.Author: prmr

57495638.Title: Limit edges on start/end nodes in State Diagrams
57495638.Author: prmr
57495638.Body: In State diagrams it should not be possible to add incoming edges to start nodes or outgoing edges from end nodes.
57495638.PlainText:
Limit edges on start/end nodes in State Diagrams

In State diagrams it should not be possible to add incoming edges to start nodes or outgoing edges from end nodes.

Comments.Body: I have addressed this bug (commit to come soon), but in my current implementation I'm not allowing ANY edges to come from an end node or to go to a start node. Would you like me to make exceptions to this new rule for Note Connector edges?
Comments.Author: JoelChev

Comments.Body: Joel's code seems good, I just fixed a bug where edges from a start to a start node were allowed.
Comments.Author: EJBQ

58971612.Title: Resolve bugs with Sequence Diagrams in Copy/Cut+Paste
58971612.Author: JoelChev
58971612.Body:
This will likely be encompassed in the larger re-design of the Diagrams and the Parent-Child relationships of their Nodes that we will be tackling later on, but for now I just want to make sure that the issue is not forgotten.

Steps to reproduce:
1. Create a new sequence diagram with a Call Edge between two ImplicitParameterNodes. 
2. Copy/Cut it.
3. Paste it.

The Call Nodes behave erratically and don't seem to be fixed properly to their lifelines. Again, this is something that will likely be addressed in a larger redesign, but I just want to make sure it is addressed.

Relevant: https://github.com/prmr/JetUML/wiki/Parent-Child-Relation
58971612.PlainText:
Resolve bugs with Sequence Diagrams in Copy/Cut+Paste

This will likely be encompassed in the larger re-design of the Diagrams and the Parent-Child relationships of their Nodes that we will be tackling later on, but for now I just want to make sure that the issue is not forgotten.
Steps to reproduce:
Create a new sequence diagram with a Call Edge between two ImplicitParameterNodes.
Copy/Cut it.
Paste it.
The Call Nodes behave erratically and don't seem to be fixed properly to their lifelines. Again, this is something that will likely be addressed in a larger redesign, but I just want to make sure it is addressed.
Relevant:

Comments.Body: I can no longer reproduce this bug. Was this fixed?
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: I'm still able to reproduce the bug following the steps above. In case it wasn't clear above, the diagram pastes properly, but the pasted diagram behaves erratically when you try to move it at all (edges and nodes don't seem to be properly fixed in place). I think a redesign of Sequence Diagrams and the relationships between nodes and edges there will likely resolve this issue.
Comments.Author: JoelChev

60819087.Title: File format incompatibility
60819087.Author: prmr
60819087.Body: There were some changes to the Graph framework that rendered the existing files unreadable (e.g., I can't load any data from the files in `/testdata`. What were these changes and how can we recover from them? I need to be able to make all the test diagram files I have work with the current version.
60819087.PlainText:
File format incompatibility

There were some changes to the Graph framework that rendered the existing files unreadable (e.g., I can't load any data from the files in /testdata. What were these changes and how can we recover from them? I need to be able to make all the test diagram files I have work with the current version.

Comments.Body: I think this was an issue with my changes to the Graph. This change preserves the changes I made and their benefits but makes the code work when the graph is not being loaded by the GraphPanel, which was an error with what I made. Saving and loading work correctly for me. @prmr, am I correct in seeing that your test files are just blank graphs except for the class file?
Comments.Author: EJBQ

Comments.Body: Yup, I think this works.
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: merged into master
Comments.Author: prmr

63045001.Author: prmr

63045001.PlainText:
Images not found in deployed jar

I created a runnable jar. It works fine but the images don't show up. The images are included in the jar, but not found by the ResourceBundle because of some sort of configuration problem. Interestingly, the license is loaded properly.

Comments.Body: The images were loaded from the project path. I'll add the calls to `getClass().getClassLoader().getResource(relativeImagePath)` and merge
Comments.Author: prmr

63046515.Title: Add a taskbar icon
63046515.Author: prmr
63046515.Body: Set up a manifest for the deployable jar so it shows a desktop/taskbar icon (a small version of the application icon).
63046515.PlainText:
Add a taskbar icon

Set up a manifest for the deployable jar so it shows a desktop/taskbar icon (a small version of the application icon).

Comments.Body: http://stackoverflow.com/questions/209812/how-do-i-change-the-default-application-icon-in-java
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: Alright, I have added a taskbar icon in the branch Issue0057. Please let me know if this looks ok, or if you want it to be different. It seems to be working fine on my end, but I would like someone else to look at it before I merge this branch into master.
Comments.Author: JoelChev

Comments.Body:
This doesn't work in deployed mode (exported as a jar). Any change involving resources should be tested in deployed mode as well. The issue is that you need to use 
`getClass().getClassLoader().getResource(...)`. Please see the comments for Issue #56.
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: Also, the application fails to load if the resource isn't found. This seems a bit brittle. It would be better to make this path recoverable, so that if the resource isn't found the application can launch anyways without the taskbar icon.
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: Thank you for the catch! I just made the path to the app.icon file relative and added a new folder to the icon folder to hold the image. I tested it in deployed mode and it seems to be working now. Can you please confirm that the changes worked? Thanks!
Comments.Author: JoelChev

Comments.Body: The change broke the About menu. When changing the path to resources, it's a good idea to do a reference check.
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: Good catch again. Thanks for looking into it. I've changed the code again, so the icon should properly display now. Thanks!
Comments.Author: JoelChev

63073855.Title: Toolbar Redesign
63073855.Author: JoelChev
63073855.Body: Move the toolbar from the top of the GraphFrame and put it on the right side. The individual buttons on each individual diagram will remain the same, but functionality will be added to expand the toolbar to give each button a label if desired.
63073855.PlainText:
Toolbar Redesign

Move the toolbar from the top of the GraphFrame and put it on the right side. The individual buttons on each individual diagram will remain the same, but functionality will be added to expand the toolbar to give each button a label if desired.

Comments.Body: I have moved the toolbar to the right side of the JInternalFrame, but I am having some issues with the labels. @prmr, do you know where the actual icons for the Toolbar buttons are located? I'm not able to find them anywhere in the project and I want to debug the text placement. Thanks!
Comments.Author: JoelChev

Comments.Body: The "icons" are drawn, not rendered. `ToolBar.add` draws a small version of the graph element.
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body:
I'm currently playing around with adding text to the buttons in the Toolbar. I do not have a toggler to add/remove the text, but I will add that later. For now I just want to see what it looks like if I add text to the buttons in the Toolbar.

There are currently some issues though. Text displays fine for pretty much all the non-node buttons, but looks horrible on the buttons for node GraphElements. After playing around with this a bit, I think the issue I'm having is Nimbus LAF related. When I go without a LAF at all, the text of every button displays properly as expected. 

Going forward, should I keep the idea of adding text to buttons, or should I put the text in a JLabel or something beside the buttons? I'm curious what you two think. I encourage you both to check out my latest commit and let me know what you think of my first shot at a design. Thanks!
Comments.Author: JoelChev

Comments.Body: The ToolBar redesign is done for now. I'm waiting for @EJBQ to look into the Persistence failures before I merge it in, but it should be good to go!
Comments.Author: JoelChev

65755087.Title: Add edge list into nodes
65755087.Author: EJBQ
65755087.Body: We need to make sure this is transient when saving, but can still be loaded back in
65755087.PlainText:
Add edge list into nodes

We need to make sure this is transient when saving, but can still be loaded back in

Comments.Body: @EJBQ I'm looking into this a bit right now. I just want to clarify the intention here a bit. As it stands, if there is an edge that goes as follows A ---> B (where A is the origin node and B is the ending node), then it is only "added" through A, it's origin. Would you like the implementation to be set up in such a way that when you add an edge it is stored in the transient ArrayList of Node A AND Node B, or just Node A? Thanks!
Comments.Author: JoelChev

Comments.Body:
Hmm. I think it would be better if it were added to both Node A and Node B. And it's simple enough to test if the node is the start or the end, so we can keep them all in one list.

Adding to both nodes let you remove a edge from the selection automatically that goes into the node you are removing, which is better.
Comments.Author: EJBQ

Comments.Body: I have implemented an initial design with two transient ArrayLists of Edges (origin and terminal edges). I can modify this if needed, but I need to do some work testing it for the time being.
Comments.Author: JoelChev

66114115.Title: Broken tests in master branch
66114115.Author: prmr
66114115.Body: A change to the persistence format broke the tests for `PersistenceService`.
66114115.PlainText:
Broken tests in master branch

A change to the persistence format broke the tests for PersistenceService.

Comments.Body:
These two commits should fix changes in persistence testing from what @JoelChev did with the node sizes. 

Reading and writing graphs appears to have been broken by the immutability of ClassRelationshipEdge, because the XMLDecoder was using the public methods to edit edges when reading them in. When trying to save it seems to be failing because of the BeanInfo change though. So I am working on those two things right now, to see if there is a workaround from having those methods public.

Comments.Body: Also of note: When saving the graph in the object diagram test, it switches the order of the fields of the empty middle object, so it goes 4-3-2, not 2-3-4.

Comments.Body:
This commit takes away the in-code immutability of the edges which was breaking the saving and loading JavaBeans code, but keeps it immutable from the user perspective and keeps the creation in static methods.

Now all persistence tests work except the object diagram, which Joel and I are looking at.

66373483.Title: Select Element After Creation
66373483.Author: prmr
66373483.Body: Currently when you want to create a model element and name it (e.g., a class), you have to create it, select the selection tool, select it, then click the name field. I have a tendency to try to do this which results in the annoying behavior that multiple classes get created. Instead, if a selection tool is active but the mouse is clicked inside an object that cannot be the parent of the created element, the object clicked should be selected and it's property editor opened instead.
66373483.PlainText:
Select Element After Creation

Currently when you want to create a model element and name it (e.g., a class), you have to create it, select the selection tool, select it, then click the name field. I have a tendency to try to do this which results in the annoying behavior that multiple classes get created. Instead, if a selection tool is active but the mouse is clicked inside an object that cannot be the parent of the created element, the object clicked should be selected and it's property editor opened instead.

Comments.Body: The focus part is handled in #69
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: I have added logic now so that if you click on a Node, it will switch the tool to be the Selection tool instead. There are currently three exceptions to this rule: PackageNodes, ObjectNodes and ImplicitParameterNodes (as these nodes can contain other nodes inside them). Now, when a user clicks on a node twice, it will display its property editor for them, with the first field selected.
Comments.Author: JoelChev

So I'm having an issue with the merge that I haven't been able to figure out after a while of debugging. 

Steps to reproduce:
1) Create a Sequence Diagram
2) Create two ImplicitParameterNodes with one CallNode each
3) Make an edge from the left CallNode to the right one
4) Copy and Paste this entire setup

Actual Output: A second child of the CallNode without an edge to it

I've followed every step here in the code and can't figure out where this is happening.

I've created a new branch Issue0067Merge which contains the merged code.

71356953.Title: Duplication of Call Nodes
71356953.Author: EJBQ
71356953.Body:
Steps to reproduce:
1) Create a Sequence Diagram
2) Create two ImplicitParameterNodes with one CallNode each
3) Make an edge from the left CallNode to the right one
4) Copy and Paste this entire setup

Expected output: An exact copy of what was copied
Actual Output: A second child of the CallNode without an edge to it. You may need to move the pasted parent call node to see the duplicated node.
71356953.PlainText:
Duplication of Call Nodes

Steps to reproduce:
Create a Sequence Diagram
Create two ImplicitParameterNodes with one CallNode each
Make an edge from the left CallNode to the right one
Copy and Paste this entire setup
Expected output: An exact copy of what was copied
Actual Output: A second child of the CallNode without an edge to it. You may need to move the pasted parent call node to see the duplicated node.

Comments.Body: The seemingly related issue of deleting and undoing a child call node duplicating the child seems to have been created in the Issue0062 branch, with the addition of edge lists in nodes. Removing that merge gets rid of that part of the issue. @JoelChev , can you look into this issue some?
Comments.Author: EJBQ

Comments.Body: Hey, thanks for finding this @EJBQ . I will look into it tomorrow evening or on Wednesday once I finish with my final exams. I will keep you and @prmr posted.
Comments.Author: JoelChev

Comments.Body:
Any movement on this?

It seems the problem is caused by the last `for` loop in `Clipboard.pasteInto(GraphPanel pGraphPanel, boolean pOriginalPositions)`. This makes a call to `Graph.connect`, and inside this call one call node is not found, and re-created. Besides the bug, this implementation appears to be inconsistent with the Javadoc of the method connect, which says that if both nodes are not found, the edge is not added. This needs to be inspected more carefully.
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: The bug seems to be resolved on my end. I just added a small if statement to skip the Graph.connect step on CallNode->CallNode edges. This seems to do the trick, although it doesn't actually solve whatever the underlying problem is. I've also refactored the Clipboard class to comply with the Checkstyle guidelines. Everything should be good to go for Milestone 1.0 now.
Comments.Author: JoelChev

72257171.Title: Extra call node creation
72257171.Author: EJBQ
72257171.Body:
This is again with sequence diagrams.

Steps to reproduce:
Create two implicit parameter nodes
Give them one call child each
Connect the call nodes
Delete the target call node
Undo the deletion

What this does, which you can check by giving the edge and node non-default properties, is create a new call node at the end of the same edge when it is put back. Then the original call node is placed below the new one. Both new call nodes are children of the original parent call node.

If you delete the commits one by one going into the past, I'm seeing this issue appear with the merge of edge lists in nodes.
72257171.PlainText:
Extra call node creation

This is again with sequence diagrams.
Steps to reproduce:
Create two implicit parameter nodes
Give them one call child each
Connect the call nodes
Delete the target call node
Undo the deletion
What this does, which you can check by giving the edge and node non-default properties, is create a new call node at the end of the same edge when it is put back. Then the original call node is placed below the new one. Both new call nodes are children of the original parent call node.
If you delete the commits one by one going into the past, I'm seeing this issue appear with the merge of edge lists in nodes.

Comments.Body: Ah, I get it. Is the callee still a child node? I thought we had refactored this away.
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body:
The callee is still a listed as part of aCalls, which is the separate storage that calls have for their children now.

So what I'm seeing with this is the edge is never added to originEdges or terminalEdges for either callNode. Then when the edge is deleted, because it is not in those lists, when removeEdge is called on the nodes, they return true, which breaks the for loop of notifying the edges.. I'm not sure in what cases that method is supposed to return true or false though.

I just pushed a change that I think fixes it, but there definitely still issues with the origin and terminal edge lists that I pointed to above. I have tested this change thoroughly yet, but it seems to work for me.
Comments.Author: EJBQ

72259346.Title: Package nodes don't draw properly
72259346.Author: prmr
72259346.Body:
Creating a package node in a class diagram with something in it, then trying to:
- Move it
- copy/paste it
- Add more stuff in it

All lead to some erratic behavior. This issue was in 0.4, and I suspect perhaps even in the original code base, but that's nevertheless something we need to fix.

When moving a package node, the outline of the node does not become visible until the node is dropped. 

In `PackageNode` the `aTop` and `aBottom`  rectangles don't get refreshed when the node is translated. They should be computed on the fly in getShape(); Similarly, when the node is snapped, the top and bottom rectangles are not translated.
72259346.PlainText:
Package nodes don't draw properly

Creating a package node in a class diagram with something in it, then trying to:
Move it
copy/paste it
Add more stuff in it
All lead to some erratic behavior. This issue was in 0.4, and I suspect perhaps even in the original code base, but that's nevertheless something we need to fix.
When moving a package node, the outline of the node does not become visible until the node is dropped.
In PackageNode the aTop and aBottom  rectangles don't get refreshed when the node is translated. They should be computed on the fly in getShape(); Similarly, when the node is snapped, the top and bottom rectangles are not translated.

Comments.Body: @prmr I realized what the middleLabel check is used for. The testEdgeEquality method is really just used to determine the type of edge that needs to be pasted into the graph. Without calling this method, the clipboard class iterates through all of the possible edges for a given diagram and pastes a copy of each (as there is no simple .equals() method right now for a given edge type). Some edges (in Use Case Diagrams for instance) have a middle label like "<<include>>" which I need to compare against the list of Edges for a given diagram.

73747149.Title: Moving entire sequence diagram moves the callnodes down and omits created nodes
73747149.Author: prmr
73747149.Body:
Steps to reproduce:
1. Create a sequence diagram with at least one call edge
2. Select the entire diagram
3. Move the entire selection right-left or down. The call nodes move down.

Additionally, if a "created" ImplicitParameterNode is part of the selection, it is not moved.
73747149.PlainText:
Moving entire sequence diagram moves the callnodes down and omits created nodes

Steps to reproduce:
Create a sequence diagram with at least one call edge
Select the entire diagram
Move the entire selection right-left or down. The call nodes move down.
Additionally, if a "created" ImplicitParameterNode is part of the selection, it is not moved.

Comments.Body:
Quick fix for solving the created nodes not moving:
- in `GraphPanelMouseMotionListener.mouseDragged` change the line:

`if(!aSelectedElements.contains(n.getParent())`

to

`if(!aSelectedElements.contains(n.getParent()) || n instanceof ImplicitParameterNode) // parents are responsible for translating their children
`

So created nodes don't get skipped,
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: This bug disappeared as the result of the #105 code refactoring.
Comments.Author: prmr

73775932.Title: Sequence: undoing a call node deletion does not put the return edge back
73775932.Author: prmr
73775932.Body:
To reproduce:
1. Create two implicit parameter nodes
2. Create a call edge between the two, with a return edge.
3. Delete the target call node.
4. undo.

95043269.Title: Simplify the parent node semantics
95043269.Author: prmr
95043269.Body:
Parent nodes should act as "master" for their children, with the following consequences:
- It should be impossible to select both a child and its parent. Selecting a parent automatically applies all operations (e.g., clone, translate) to the children.
- The graph object should only track root nodes (nodes with a `null` parent), as all other nodes can be tracked through their parent.
- Drawing children nodes should be done recursively through their parent
- Cloning children nodes should be done recursively through their parent
- Translating children nodes should be done recursively through their parent
- Copying a node and pasting it should paste it back to its original parent, the only catch with that is that for package nodes, it would be impossible to cut a node from inside the package and copy it outside. For this we would need some sort of 'detach' operation, or take into account the position of the mouse when the command is issued.
95043269.PlainText:
Simplify the parent node semantics

Parent nodes should act as "master" for their children, with the following consequences:
It should be impossible to select both a child and its parent. Selecting a parent automatically applies all operations (e.g., clone, translate) to the children.
The graph object should only track root nodes (nodes with a null parent), as all other nodes can be tracked through their parent.
Drawing children nodes should be done recursively through their parent
Cloning children nodes should be done recursively through their parent
Translating children nodes should be done recursively through their parent
Copying a node and pasting it should paste it back to its original parent, the only catch with that is that for package nodes, it would be impossible to cut a node from inside the package and copy it outside. For this we would need some sort of 'detach' operation, or take into account the position of the mouse when the command is issued.

Events.Author: prmr
Events.RenamedFrom: Make it impossible to select a node and its children
Events.RenamedTo: Simplify the parent node semantics

103773438.Title: Overlapping Edges in Diagrams
103773438.Author: prmr
103773438.Body:
In all diagrams except sequence diagrams, adding multiple edges between two nodes invisibly stacks the edges. For class, object, state, and use case diagrams, this should not be possible. For sequence diagrams, the edge is re-drawn lower and lower: this should be fixed.
At the same time, the arrow end properties should be editable to make it possible to have bidirectional edges. We could even consider adding a bidirectional edge prototype to the toolbar.
103773438.PlainText:
Overlapping Edges in Diagrams

In all diagrams except sequence diagrams, adding multiple edges between two nodes invisibly stacks the edges. For class, object, state, and use case diagrams, this should not be possible. For sequence diagrams, the edge is re-drawn lower and lower: this should be fixed.
At the same time, the arrow end properties should be editable to make it possible to have bidirectional edges. We could even consider adding a bidirectional edge prototype to the toolbar.

Comments.Body: There is quite a bit of work to do to improve the edges management, including creating specialized classes for aggregation, dependency, etc.
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: With the new design for the edge collection we can simply constrain diagrams (except sequence diagrams) to have a single type of edge between two nodes (one per direction).
Comments.Author: prmr

110004516.Title: Smarter detection of graph dirty status
110004516.Author: prmr
110004516.Body:
Currently sequences of operations that do not change the status of a graph still result in a graph getting flagged as "dirty" (i.e., with unsaved modifications). These include:
- Undoing all operations 

It would be best if the tool could detect cases where no real modifications have been done to avoid flagging the graph as dirty.
110004516.PlainText:
Smarter detection of graph dirty status

Currently sequences of operations that do not change the status of a graph still result in a graph getting flagged as "dirty" (i.e., with unsaved modifications). These include:
Undoing all operations
It would be best if the tool could detect cases where no real modifications have been done to avoid flagging the graph as dirty.

Comments.Body: Solved by moving all the logic to determine whether a diagram has unmodified changes to `DiagramOperationProcessor` and delegating the required calls to this class. The current solution does not show visual feedback about whether a diagram is saved or not. Doing so would require another instance of the Observer to allow `DiagramTab` to listen to changes in `DiagramOperationProcessor`. It's not clear that the feature is worth this increase in code structures.
Comments.Author: prmr

113952771.Title: Simplify the Edge Hierarchy
113952771.Author: prmr
113952771.Body:
- `ShapedEdge` can be pulled up into `AbstractEdge`, and the `getShape` method should be made protected for a true implementation of the Template Method Design Pattern.
- The start, middle, and end label properties can be removed from the `SegmentedEdge` because they are not used for `CallEdge` and `ReturnEdge`.
- `getShape` can be used uniformly to draw the edge
- `ClassRelationshipEdge` can be split into multiple types and the visual properties encoded as class-instance fields. The segmentation algorithms should be implemented as strategies.

and so on.
113952771.PlainText:
Simplify the Edge Hierarchy

ShapedEdge can be pulled up into AbstractEdge, and the getShape method should be made protected for a true implementation of the Template Method Design Pattern.
The start, middle, and end label properties can be removed from the SegmentedEdge because they are not used for CallEdge and ReturnEdge.
getShape can be used uniformly to draw the edge
ClassRelationshipEdge can be split into multiple types and the visual properties encoded as class-instance fields. The segmentation algorithms should be implemented as strategies.
and so on.

It should not be possible to create a return edge that both starts and ends on the same activation box.

118836964.Title: Creating an ImplicitParameterNode on an empty Diagram should include a CallNode

118844520.Title: Editable Final Property on Circular States Can Break Diagram Semantics
118844520.Author: prmr
118844520.Body: There is a rule preventing edges into initial states and out of final states, but since it's possible to edit the `final` property of the state, these constraints can be violated. The property should not be user-editable.
118844520.PlainText:
Editable Final Property on Circular States Can Break Diagram Semantics

There is a rule preventing edges into initial states and out of final states, but since it's possible to edit the final property of the state, these constraints can be violated. The property should not be user-editable.

124151713.Title: Support multiple connection points per node side in Class Diagram
124151713.Author: prmr
124151713.Body: When more than one edge connects to a connection point, the resulting edge can be confounded and difficult to see. Different edges should attach to different connection points on a node.
124151713.PlainText:
Support multiple connection points per node side in Class Diagram

When more than one edge connects to a connection point, the resulting edge can be confounded and difficult to see. Different edges should attach to different connection points on a node.

Comments.Body: One solution would be to change `Node.getConnectionPoint` so that it returns a connection points that takes into account the relative position of other nodes w.r.t. the implicit parameter. Unfortunately, that would also require the implicit parameter Node object somehow obtaining a reference to the `Graph` that contains it, which would induce a cycle of dependencies. It might be tolerable to keep this around only in concrete `Node` classes that need the information.
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: Considering passing a `Graph` argument to `Edge.draw`. Experiment with `DependencyEdge`
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: The self-edge needs to be dynamic as well
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: `SegmentationStyle.getPath` needs to take the edge as argument, otherwise the `Position` calculation is too difficult to implement for pairs of nodes that have different types of edges between them.
Comments.Author: prmr

127055409.Title: Sequence Diagram Self-Call Node Selection
127055409.Author: prmr
127055409.Body: In Sequence diagram with self-calls, when attempting to select a self-call node that is overlaid on top of its caller node, if the mouse is pressed at a point that is on the left side of the leaf node (so also overlapping the caller), then the caller will be selected instead of the leaf callee. The workaround is to ensure to select a point on the right of the caller node when selecting leaf called nodes. This should be fixed as part of an overhaul that adds a Z coordinate to nodes to properly manage who's on top.
127055409.PlainText:
Sequence Diagram Self-Call Node Selection

In Sequence diagram with self-calls, when attempting to select a self-call node that is overlaid on top of its caller node, if the mouse is pressed at a point that is on the left side of the leaf node (so also overlapping the caller), then the caller will be selected instead of the leaf callee. The workaround is to ensure to select a point on the right of the caller node when selecting leaf called nodes. This should be fixed as part of an overhaul that adds a Z coordinate to nodes to properly manage who's on top.

127055490.Title: Point node deletion, selection and translation
127055490.Author: prmr
127055490.Body:
It's not possible to select a point node using the rubberband drag gesture, so the corresponding note edge is also not selected. When selecting a note edge (and its attached node) with the multi-selection gesture and attempting to translate the group, only the note node moves, not the point node.

Point nodes also don't get deleted when the corresponding note edge is deleted, so they get selected with the Select All feature.
127055490.PlainText:
Point node deletion, selection and translation

It's not possible to select a point node using the rubberband drag gesture, so the corresponding note edge is also not selected. When selecting a note edge (and its attached node) with the multi-selection gesture and attempting to translate the group, only the note node moves, not the point node.
Point nodes also don't get deleted when the corresponding note edge is deleted, so they get selected with the Select All feature.

Comments.Body: The problem was simply that Rectangle.contains(Rectangle r) returns false is r has 0 width and height. Weird. Fixed by giving point nodes a tiny dimension.
Comments.Author: prmr

Events.RenamedTo: Point node deletion, selection and translation

129733588.PlainText:
Re-size objects not possible.

143263543.Title: Pasting partial sequence diagrams leads to crashes
143263543.Author: prmr
143263543.Body:
Steps to reproduce:
1. Create a sequence diagram with at least one call edge between two implicit parameter nodes
2. Select only the call edges and call nodes.
3. Copy the selection.
4. Open a new sequence diagram graph
5. Paste the selection

The application will hang.

The problem generalizes to pasting children nodes without their parent.
143263543.PlainText:
Pasting partial sequence diagrams leads to crashes

Steps to reproduce:
Create a sequence diagram with at least one call edge between two implicit parameter nodes
Select only the call edges and call nodes.
Copy the selection.
Open a new sequence diagram graph
Paste the selection
The application will hang.
The problem generalizes to pasting children nodes without their parent.

Comments.Body: Problem 1: The graph area grow/clip feature relied on a null value.
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: Problem 2: The paste feature pasted children nodes with a reference to a parent node, when the parent node was not in the clip board. If children nodes are pasted without their parent, and this is a legal subgraph, the links to the (obsolete) parents should be clipped so the nodes can be made root nodes of the destination graph.
Comments.Author: prmr

143497221.Title: Refactor graph creation behavior to distinguish between validated and non-validated operations
143497221.Author: prmr
143497221.Body:
There are clearly two scenarios for adding nodes and edges to a graph: operations that must be validated and possibly complemented (i.e., operations done by the user through the GUI), and operations that are simply reconstruction of a graph for stored versions (e.g., from persisted graphs or from copy-pasting operations). Currently the two modes are tangled, but the code would be cleaner if they were separated.
Try to see if as part of this we can solve the problem of ObjectGraph nodes getting pasted/undeleted back in alphabetical order, independent of the initial ordering (which should be preserved).
143497221.PlainText:
Refactor graph creation behavior to distinguish between validated and non-validated operations

There are clearly two scenarios for adding nodes and edges to a graph: operations that must be validated and possibly complemented (i.e., operations done by the user through the GUI), and operations that are simply reconstruction of a graph for stored versions (e.g., from persisted graphs or from copy-pasting operations). Currently the two modes are tangled, but the code would be cleaner if they were separated.
Try to see if as part of this we can solve the problem of ObjectGraph nodes getting pasted/undeleted back in alphabetical order, independent of the initial ordering (which should be preserved).

162711860.Title: Solve Differing Geometry Question between Win/Linux platforms
162711860.Author: prmr
162711860.Body: The tests don't pass on Linux due to slight differences in the calculation of geometric positions. Why?
162711860.PlainText:
Solve Differing Geometry Question between Win/Linux platforms

The tests don't pass on Linux due to slight differences in the calculation of geometric positions. Why?

Comments.Body:
Two Possible Cause: 
1. Different Fonts used in different system. Even for Windows, some test cases failed on Windows 8.1 Chinese edition but all test are passed on Windows 10 English Edition. https://docs.oracle.com/javase/tutorial/uiswing/layout/problems.html
2. Different Rendering techniques used by different systems. 
   http://cs.nyu.edu/~yap/classes/visual/03s/lect/l7/
Comments.Author: jc-chen157

Comments.Body: For Windows System, right click Project -> Properties -> Resource. Change Text File encoding to UTF-8 should fix the issues. Still trying to figure out how to fix for Mac and Linux.
Comments.Author: jc-chen157

Comments.Body: The issue has been confirmed to be related to the font size. To solve the issue, we will no longer rely on the default system font, but hard-code a font to be used by the application.
Comments.Author: prmr

215887613.Title: Undo feature reverses call order after deletion in sequence diagram
215887613.Author: aoldershaw
215887613.Body:
# Description
"Undoing" the deletion of an activation bar with more than one call in a sequence diagram results in the order of the calls being reversed. The same result occurs upon the deletion of an entire implicit parameter with an activation bar containing more than one call.

# Steps to Reproduce
1. Open a new JetUML Sequence diagram
2. Create 2 implicit parameters, A and B, say. Create two calls from A to B, call1() and call2(), say, originating from the **same** activation bar. The diagram should look similar to this:
![diagram1](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/26583442/24172154/9036858c-0e5d-11e7-957d-525d05927e2a.png)
3. Select the activation bar on A, and delete it (Edit -> Delete)
4. Undo the deletion (Edit -> Undo). The diagram should now look similar to this:
![diagram2](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/26583442/24172210/c8d15cc8-0e5d-11e7-8410-45fbc9cad749.png)
215887613.PlainText:
Undo feature reverses call order after deletion in sequence diagram

"Undoing" the deletion of an activation bar with more than one call in a sequence diagram results in the order of the calls being reversed. The same result occurs upon the deletion of an entire implicit parameter with an activation bar containing more than one call.

Create 2 implicit parameters, A and B, say. Create two calls from A to B, call1() and call2(), say, originating from the same activation bar. The diagram should look similar to this:
Select the activation bar on A, and delete it (Edit -> Delete)
Undo the deletion (Edit -> Undo). The diagram should now look similar to this:

241759879.Title: Move draw method to separate hierarchy
241759879.Author: prmr
241759879.Body:
Move the `Edge.draw(...)` method out of the `Edge` interface and into a new interface `EdgeRenderer` in charge of drawing edges. Instead, edges should return their renderer.

Requirements:
* Some layouts require a global analysis of the graph.
* Some layouts allow multiple edges of the same types between nodes.
* Some layouts require knowing how many edges are attached to different sides of a node
* Layouts should be aware of the grid that they can snap to.
* The design should allow pluggable contributions (i.e. for people to add new layout algorithms).

Another desirable characteristic is for a design that allow incremental introduction of layouts into the existing code.
241759879.PlainText:
Move draw method to separate hierarchy

Move the Edge.draw(...) method out of the Edge interface and into a new interface EdgeRenderer in charge of drawing edges. Instead, edges should return their renderer.
Requirements:
Some layouts require a global analysis of the graph.
Some layouts allow multiple edges of the same types between nodes.
Some layouts require knowing how many edges are attached to different sides of a node
Layouts should be aware of the grid that they can snap to.
The design should allow pluggable contributions (i.e. for people to add new layout algorithms).
Another desirable characteristic is for a design that allow incremental introduction of layouts into the existing code.

274182197.Title: Separate Data and View concerns in Edge Hierarchy
274182197.Author: prmr
274182197.Body: In the current design `Edge` classes both support the basic representation of an edge in terms of start and end node, and provide all the logic to draw the edge. The edge drawing logic needs to be moved to a separate class, to facilitate transitioning to a new GUI framework. In the new design, `Edge` classes will supply a wrapper capable of drawing them using the call `edge.view()`, so e.g., to get the bounds of an edge one would do `edge.view().getBounds()` instead of `edge.getBounds()`.
274182197.PlainText:
Separate Data and View concerns in Edge Hierarchy

In the current design Edge classes both support the basic representation of an edge in terms of start and end node, and provide all the logic to draw the edge. The edge drawing logic needs to be moved to a separate class, to facilitate transitioning to a new GUI framework. In the new design, Edge classes will supply a wrapper capable of drawing them using the call edge.view(), so e.g., to get the bounds of an edge one would do edge.view().getBounds() instead of edge.getBounds().

291559311.Title: PointNode does not auto-delete when note edge deleted
291559311.Author: prmr
291559311.Body:
When deleting a note edge connected to a Point node, the Point node does not get deleted. This is the default behavior for all nodes. However, in the case of a Point node, it makes no sense to leave it in, so it should be deleted whenever its corresponding note edge is deleted.

**Steps to reproduce**:
1. Create a new class diagram
2. Create a Class Node and a Note node
3. Create a new note edge by starting from the note node and ending at an arbitrary location (not within the class node)
4. Select only the edge and delete it
5. Select All

The selection will show the remaining point node.
291559311.PlainText:
PointNode does not auto-delete when note edge deleted

When deleting a note edge connected to a Point node, the Point node does not get deleted. This is the default behavior for all nodes. However, in the case of a Point node, it makes no sense to leave it in, so it should be deleted whenever its corresponding note edge is deleted.
Steps to reproduce:
Create a new class diagram
Create a Class Node and a Note node
Create a new note edge by starting from the note node and ending at an arbitrary location (not within the class node)
Select only the edge and delete it
Select All
The selection will show the remaining point node.

Comments.Body: In `Graph.removeEdge(Edge)`, `if( n instanceof NoteEdge )` should be `if( n instanceof NoteNode)`.
Comments.Author: prmr
