295787218.Title: Display too small on screen in 3000*2000 in 13inch
295787218.Author: LotuxPunk
295787218.Body:
Hello, 
I have a Surface Book, and my screen has a large resolution on a 13-inch screen, so the different elements in the interface look small. An option to adjust that? 

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/16562647/36017652-ed7eba82-0d78-11e8-830d-af24e67cafc6.png)

![2018-02-09](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/16562647/36017920-fc6963fc-0d79-11e8-8f05-daeb57677924.png)
295787218.PlainText:
Display too small on screen in 3000*2000 in 13inch

Hello,
I have a Surface Book, and my screen has a large resolution on a 13-inch screen, so the different elements in the interface look small. An option to adjust that?

Comments.Body:
This is a known issue caused by the fact that the current GUI library used (Swing) does not support HD screens. We are actively working on this with a port to JavaFX (#232 ), which will go into Release 2.0, scheduled for the Spring. In the meantime, two workarounds include:
* Using the Zoom feature (`View | Zoom In`) which at least makes the diagrams easier to see.
* (For the more adventurous) check out branch `Integration-JavaFX`, which already has the outer frame and dialogs ported, and will progressively become more HD-Screen friendly over the next few weeks. This will require compiling your own jar, but it should build out of the box in e.g., Eclipse.
Comments.Author: prmr

Events.Author: prmr
Events.RenamedFrom: [UI] Display too small on screen in 3000*2000 in 13inch
Events.RenamedTo: Display too small on screen in 3000*2000 in 13inch

308789101.Title: Add Scrollbars in JavaFX UI
308789101.Author: kkutschera
308789101.Body:
In pull request #237, attempts were made to add scrollbars. In the last commit, https://github.com/prmr/JetUML/pull/237/commits/e41a80f55ea37b209d147cc24e280c0682c0693b, the following issues were still unresolved:
1) `ScrollPane` Viewing Area when `Canvas` is Resized
The `ScrollPane` viewing area is controlled by the scrollbar’s `hValue` and `vValue` properties. These properties are numbers between 0 and 1 that control the position of the scrollbars (for example, `hValue==1` means the rightmost position). Because of this, if `hValue==1` then the viewing area follows the rightmost edge of the canvas, otherwise the viewing area looks at whichever area is corresponding to the current `hValue` and `vValue` properties. This can be awkward, for example, if you set the scrollbar’s values to 1 and drag a node to the bottom-right corner of the viewing area, the viewing area follows, but if you take another node and drag it up to the top-left corner the viewing area, it doesn’t follow. From further research there doesn't seem to be a reliable way to get the viewport location and size of the `ScrollPane` since the provided `viewportBounds` property is not reliably updated when the viewport changes (it appears to only update if the window is resized and not if the scrollbars are moved)
2) Node Jumping to Meet Mouse
When dragging a node that is responsible for determining the graph bounds into the original viewing area of the `ScrollPane`, when the scrollbars disappear the node will jump to meet the position of the mouse (that is if the scrollbars are following the node’s position).
3) Responsiveness/Lag
There is more lag than with the Swing UI, which is believed to be due to canvas size.  The `Canvas` has to be created with a maximum size otherwise it does not appear at all.

Additionally, note that in https://github.com/prmr/JetUML/pull/237/commits/fec8554dbbbf6465cd4755f88f86f72d7708456b logic was added so that when the user drags the node past the canvas and drags it back there is no disconnect between the mouse location and the node location on the canvas.
308789101.PlainText:
Add Scrollbars in JavaFX UI

In pull request #237, attempts were made to add scrollbars. In the last commit, , the following issues were still unresolved:
ScrollPane Viewing Area when Canvas is Resized
The ScrollPane viewing area is controlled by the scrollbar’s hValue and vValue properties. These properties are numbers between 0 and 1 that control the position of the scrollbars (for example, hValue==1 means the rightmost position). Because of this, if hValue==1 then the viewing area follows the rightmost edge of the canvas, otherwise the viewing area looks at whichever area is corresponding to the current hValue and vValue properties. This can be awkward, for example, if you set the scrollbar’s values to 1 and drag a node to the bottom-right corner of the viewing area, the viewing area follows, but if you take another node and drag it up to the top-left corner the viewing area, it doesn’t follow. From further research there doesn't seem to be a reliable way to get the viewport location and size of the ScrollPane since the provided viewportBounds property is not reliably updated when the viewport changes (it appears to only update if the window is resized and not if the scrollbars are moved)
Node Jumping to Meet Mouse
When dragging a node that is responsible for determining the graph bounds into the original viewing area of the ScrollPane, when the scrollbars disappear the node will jump to meet the position of the mouse (that is if the scrollbars are following the node’s position).
Responsiveness/Lag
There is more lag than with the Swing UI, which is believed to be due to canvas size.  The Canvas has to be created with a maximum size otherwise it does not appear at all.
Additionally, note that in  logic was added so that when the user drags the node past the canvas and drags it back there is no disconnect between the mouse location and the node location on the canvas.

Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: I don't foresee any problems with that plan. Since the scrollbars are based on the size of the window, you shouldn't get any viewport issues like we had when trying to dynamically change the canvas size.
Comments.Author: kkutschera

Comments.Body:
There is till work left to do on this one, namely:
* Automatically pan the scrollpane viewport as a selection is dragged outside the visible boundaries;
* Allow the user to set the canvas size.

However, these improvements should be open as new issues, since they will be much easier to implement after #257 is done.
Comments.Author: prmr

312481631.Author: kkutschera

When opening a diagram for the first time the toolbar jitters. This may be caused by the computation of preferred sizes when a `GraphFrame` is first created.

This issue is expanded to generally improve the Toolbar class, to:
* Take advantage of the new JavaFX `ToolBar` class
* Refactor the code, reduce state
* Eliminate the jitters upon initialization
* Improve the look and feel and give it a more modern look
* Select whether to view the tool hints or not through the View menu instead of an expansion button.
* Persist the view options #243

322393274.Title: Monitor selection in dual-screen setup
322393274.Author: nassifm
322393274.Body: When using JetUML on a dual-screen setup, with the main window being on the second monitor, all dialogs and right-click menu appears in the first monitor, instead of where they should.
322393274.PlainText:
Monitor selection in dual-screen setup

When using JetUML on a dual-screen setup, with the main window being on the second monitor, all dialogs and right-click menu appears in the first monitor, instead of where they should.

Comments.Body:
For the popup menu, the issue is caused by a mismatch between scene and screen coordinates. 

Method `DiagramFrameToolBar.showPopup` takes in a point argument supplied in scene coordinates and passes it to `ContextMenu,show` which expects a point in screen coordinates. Same with the property sheet.

For the properties sheet, the issue is a lack of API documentation.

It seems (?) that by default creating a new `Stage` will center it on the primary screen. The documentation of the `Stage.centerOnScreen()` seems to be inaccurate because inserting a call to it when creating the property sheet still does not position it on the "current" screen, whatever that is.
Comments.Author: prmr

330704577.Title: Fix Spurious Scrollbars
330704577.Author: prmr
330704577.Body:
When the application window is resized so that the diagram is not entirely visible in one dimension (say, width), scrollbars will appear in both dimensions. 

This problem is the result of the conscious design decision to use `ScrollPane.setMaxSize(...)` in `DiagramTab`. Because the ScrollPane's size is bounded, the extra space occupied by the scrollbar will cause the viewport to shrink in the other dimension and the max size property, required for centering, will prevent allowing more space for it. 

The real issue here is that it turns out to be extremely non-obvious to find an elegant solution to center a non-resizable node (the `DiagramCanvas`) wrapped in a `ScrollPane` as the center node of a `BorderPane`.

The desired behavior is that a diagram canvas that is smaller than the scrollpane viewport would flow naturally to the center of the available tab pane, and become scrollable if the window is resized to a size smaller than the canvas.

Unfortunately, even if the node wrapped by the `ScrollPane` is non-resizable, the `ScrollPane` will still expand to fill the entire available space of the `BorderPane` center, which invariably positions the diagram canvas to the top left of the tab pane. 

The issue is not judged to be severe enough to pollute the code with hard-nosed solutions involving listeners or programmatic layouting. 

The desired solution should be limited to a clean layout configuration in the constructor of `DiagramTab`
330704577.PlainText:
Fix Spurious Scrollbars

When the application window is resized so that the diagram is not entirely visible in one dimension (say, width), scrollbars will appear in both dimensions.
This problem is the result of the conscious design decision to use ScrollPane.setMaxSize(...) in DiagramTab. Because the ScrollPane's size is bounded, the extra space occupied by the scrollbar will cause the viewport to shrink in the other dimension and the max size property, required for centering, will prevent allowing more space for it.
The real issue here is that it turns out to be extremely non-obvious to find an elegant solution to center a non-resizable node (the DiagramCanvas) wrapped in a ScrollPane as the center node of a BorderPane.
The desired behavior is that a diagram canvas that is smaller than the scrollpane viewport would flow naturally to the center of the available tab pane, and become scrollable if the window is resized to a size smaller than the canvas.
Unfortunately, even if the node wrapped by the ScrollPane is non-resizable, the ScrollPane will still expand to fill the entire available space of the BorderPane center, which invariably positions the diagram canvas to the top left of the tab pane.
The issue is not judged to be severe enough to pollute the code with hard-nosed solutions involving listeners or programmatic layouting.
The desired solution should be limited to a clean layout configuration in the constructor of DiagramTab

337502791.Title: Model-View-Controller Refactoring
337502791.Author: prmr
337502791.Body:
Split the diagram construction logic into a different class so that we have three class hierarchies to address the three main concerns with diagrams:
* `Diagram`: Stores the logical structure of the diagram only. Indepedent of any geometry or GUI
* `DiagramBuilder`: Business rules to create diagram from various interactions with a Canvas. 
* `DiagramViewer`: Responsible for computing the geometry of the diagram (through delegation to nodes)
337502791.PlainText:
Model-View-Controller Refactoring

Split the diagram construction logic into a different class so that we have three class hierarchies to address the three main concerns with diagrams:
Diagram: Stores the logical structure of the diagram only. Indepedent of any geometry or GUI
DiagramBuilder: Business rules to create diagram from various interactions with a Canvas.
DiagramViewer: Responsible for computing the geometry of the diagram (through delegation to nodes)

352693800.Title: Centering of Object diagram name incorrect with long value field.
352693800.Author: prmr
352693800.Body:
In Release 2.1 with Java 1.8.0_171 on Windows 10.

The name of objects in Object diagram does not center properly when the value of one of more field names is too long.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Create an new Object diagram
2. Create an new object node in the diagram
3. Name the node "Foo"
4. Add a field to the object node.
5. Change the value of the `value` properties of the field node to `XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX`

The name of the node will be justified to the left instead of being centered.

## Root Cause

In `ObjectNodeView.draw`, the bounds of the top rectangle and the entire object don't have the same width.
352693800.PlainText:
Centering of Object diagram name incorrect with long value field.

In Release 2.1 with Java 1.8.0_171 on Windows 10.
The name of objects in Object diagram does not center properly when the value of one of more field names is too long.
Steps to reproduce

Create an new object node in the diagram
Name the node "Foo"
Add a field to the object node.
Change the value of the value properties of the field node to XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX
The name of the node will be justified to the left instead of being centered.
Root Cause
In ObjectNodeView.draw, the bounds of the top rectangle and the entire object don't have the same width.

382035540.Title: Diagram too large error prevents diagram opening
382035540.Author: neilernst
382035540.Body: **Description** Diagram too large for view dialog does not open diagram, but forces you to resize then re-open. **Steps to reproduce** 1. Open a diagram saved on a higher resolution screen. 2. Error dialog (attached) shows dimensions are wrong and suggests resetting canvas size. 3. JetUML then refuses to open diagram. **Screenshots** **Configuration** - JetUML Release: 2.2 - OS + version: Mac 10.14 - Java version 1.8 **Additional information** I expected the tool to automatically resize the canvas and add scroll bars. Since it knows the solution, it seems like the tool should just go ahead and change. This is a particular problem when working on a laptop and opening multiple student projects, some of which are saved on larger screens.
382035540.PlainText:
Diagram too large error prevents diagram opening

Description
Diagram too large for view dialog does not open diagram, but forces you to resize then re-open.
Steps to reproduce
Open a diagram saved on a higher resolution screen.
Error dialog (attached) shows dimensions are wrong and suggests resetting canvas size.
JetUML then refuses to open diagram.
Screenshots
Configuration
JetUML Release: 2.2
OS + version: Mac 10.14
Java version 1.8
Additional information
I expected the tool to automatically resize the canvas and add scroll bars. Since it knows the solution, it seems like the tool should just go ahead and change.
This is a particular problem when working on a laptop and opening multiple student projects, some of which are saved on larger screens.

Comments.Body:
This is a good suggestion. The rationale for the current behavior is twofold:
1. Canvas is not resizable
2. The size of the canvas was supposed to be a "view" attribute (in the sense of MVC), whereas the persistence feature was persisting only the model (i.e., the essential diagram components).

Given the annoyance that this causes, it might make sense to add the view size to the persistence file, and auto-size to it. 

The only issue is that storing the dimension of the canvas used to create a diagram will seriously uglify the design, since commands and open/save operate on a `Diagram`, not a `DiagramCanvas` or a `DiagramView`. Would it be acceptable to open the diagram with the minimum size required, plus a margin?
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: that seems like the right approach to me - but how is minimum size defined? I think that the default (using Photoshop as an example) would be either "1:1" (and nodes might be off screen) or "fit", and for large diagrams, you see all the nodes, but perhaps at tiny sizes.
Comments.Author: neilernst

Comments.Body:
The (minimum) bounds are generated dynamically, using
```
Rectangle bounds = DiagramType.newViewInstanceFor(diagram).getBounds();
```
So that will be the minimum size. I'll add a margin to the left/bottom
Comments.Author: prmr

385053883.Title: Unable to paste any subgraph containing a PointNode
385053883.Author: kkutschera
385053883.Body:
**Description**
Unable to paste any subgraph containing a PointNode.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Open any new diagram
2. Create a Note and attach a NoteEdge
3. Select the Note and attached NoteEdge
4. Copy and paste -> selection is not pasted

**Suspected Cause**
In the Clipboard, validNodeFor() is called to check if a paste is valid. Unfortunately, this only checks to see if the Node is contained in the diagram's node prototypes which PointNode is not included in for any diagram type.
385053883.PlainText:
Unable to paste any subgraph containing a PointNode

Description
Unable to paste any subgraph containing a PointNode.
Steps to reproduce
Open any new diagram
Create a Note and attach a NoteEdge
Select the Note and attached NoteEdge
Copy and paste -> selection is not pasted
Suspected Cause
In the Clipboard, validNodeFor() is called to check if a paste is valid. Unfortunately, this only checks to see if the Node is contained in the diagram's node prototypes which PointNode is not included in for any diagram type.

Comments.Body: Good catch. We could just return `true` for `PointNode` as the first statement. This oversight points to the need for better documentation and tests for `validPaste`
Comments.Author: prmr

403468445.Title: Exception when dragging after ctrl+click on selected node
403468445.Author: aoldershaw
403468445.Body:
**Description**
With a single node selected, if you attempt to drag on that node while holding the ctrl button (which deselects a node), a `java.util.NoSuchElementException` is thrown in the "JavaFX Application Thread".

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Create new class diagram 
2. Create a class node

Description

Create new class diagram
Create a class node
Select the class node
While holding the ctrl key, click on the class node and drag the mouse

With assertions disabled, the following exception is thrown:
The contract of SelectionModel.getSelectionBounds is broken (in particular, the assertion assert lastSelected.isPresent())

411928551.Title: Refactor Creation of Tool Icons
411928551.Author: prmr
411928551.Body:
The toolbar and context menu show icons of the nodes and edges supported by a diagram. These images are created by drawing the tool's corresponding view. There are two problems with the current design:
* The view code requires special cases just to deal with icons (e.g., `CallEdgeView`)
* The `ImageCreator` creates an image from a `Canvas`, which is suboptimal because in JavaFX it is possible to just pass the `Canvas` itself as an icon in a menu.

**Refactoring**: Create an `IconCreator` interface extended by the view interfaces and a special method in the views, and use that to create icons instead of `View.draw(...)`. 

**Note**: Some widgets, and in particular the context menu, disposes of its images when it's closed, so any reference to an image passed to it cannot be shared.
411928551.PlainText:
Refactor Creation of Tool Icons

The toolbar and context menu show icons of the nodes and edges supported by a diagram. These images are created by drawing the tool's corresponding view. There are two problems with the current design:
The view code requires special cases just to deal with icons (e.g., CallEdgeView)
The ImageCreator creates an image from a Canvas, which is suboptimal because in JavaFX it is possible to just pass the Canvas itself as an icon in a menu.
Refactoring: Create an IconCreator interface extended by the view interfaces and a special method in the views, and use that to create icons instead of View.draw(...).
Note: Some widgets, and in particular the context menu, disposes of its images when it's closed, so any reference to an image passed to it cannot be shared.

412044998.Title: JavaVersionNotDetected when using Early Access build of Java
412044998.Author: pashakondratyev
412044998.Body:
**Description**
JetUML will not startup if using an Early Access Build of Java
"Caused by: ca.mcgill.cs.jetuml.application.JavaVersionNotDetectedException: Cannot parse version string: 1.8.0_202-ea"

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Have an Early Access Build of Java
2. Try to run JetUML

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 2.3
 - OS + version: Mac OS 10.14.1
 - Java version - 1.8.202-ea
 
**Additional information**
The issue has to do with the "parseWithOldNumberingScheme" method in the JavaVersion class as when the string "1.8.202-ea" is parsed the third token becomes 202-ea which cannot be read as an integer.
412044998.PlainText:
JavaVersionNotDetected when using Early Access build of Java

Description

"Caused by: ca.mcgill.cs.jetuml.application.JavaVersionNotDetectedException: Cannot parse version string: 1.8.0_202-ea"

Have an Early Access Build of Java
Try to run JetUML
Configuration
JetUML Release: 2.3
OS + version: Mac OS 10.14.1
Java version - 1.8.202-ea
Additional information
The issue has to do with the "parseWithOldNumberingScheme" method in the JavaVersion class as when the string "1.8.202-ea" is parsed the third token becomes 202-ea which cannot be read as an integer.

430033075.Title: Simplify Dependencies in Class Diagrams
430033075.Author: prmr
430033075.Body:
Dependencies should:
- [X] Not be allowed as self-edges
- [X] Offer support for bi-directionality
- [X] Not be individually settable between two given nodes, in both directions.
- [X] Have a single label
- [X] Use the default connection point


*This is a sub-task of #230. One issue with this refactoring is that adding a property (navigability) to dependency edges will make the file format backward-incompatible, which needs to be handled carefully.*
430033075.PlainText:
Simplify Dependencies in Class Diagrams

Dependencies should:
[x] Not be allowed as self-edges
[x] Offer support for bi-directionality
[x] Not be individually settable between two given nodes, in both directions.
[x] Have a single label
[x] Use the default connection point
This is a sub-task of #230. One issue with this refactoring is that adding a property (navigability) to dependency edges will make the file format backward-incompatible, which needs to be handled carefully.

430036596.Title: Detect Version Mismatches
430036596.Author: prmr
430036596.Body:
Diagrams loaded from a version that is not the current version should be automatically updated to the newest version, including transformed if necessary. The user should be informed of this so they are given an opportunity to save the file with a different name if they want.

Note that an alert is only shown if the diagram was actually transformed for the migration (as opposed to just have its version information updated). This is because if the diagram is not transformed, it remains compatible with previous versions of JetUML.
430036596.PlainText:
Detect Version Mismatches

Diagrams loaded from a version that is not the current version should be automatically updated to the newest version, including transformed if necessary. The user should be informed of this so they are given an opportunity to save the file with a different name if they want.
Note that an alert is only shown if the diagram was actually transformed for the migration (as opposed to just have its version information updated). This is because if the diagram is not transformed, it remains compatible with previous versions of JetUML.

457619385.Title: Use UTF-8 encoding when saving diagrams
457619385.Author: prmr
457619385.Body:
Diagrams are currently saved using the default system encoding, which can lead to ambiguity when sharing diagrams across system. Change the persistence service to explicitly encode/decode using UTF-8.

This change will impacted previously saved diagrams that were saved in a format other than UTF-8, and which contain special characters (such as the French quotes used for UML stereotypes). The workaround will be to externally change the encoding.

**Migration notes:** Any diagram saved with a version 2.1-2.5 that contains non-ASCII characters can be made compatible simply by changing the encoding with the help of a text editor. The easiest way to do this is to create a new file set with UTF-8 encoding, and paste the content of the old file in.
457619385.PlainText:
Use UTF-8 encoding when saving diagrams

Diagrams are currently saved using the default system encoding, which can lead to ambiguity when sharing diagrams across system. Change the persistence service to explicitly encode/decode using UTF-8.
This change will impacted previously saved diagrams that were saved in a format other than UTF-8, and which contain special characters (such as the French quotes used for UML stereotypes). The workaround will be to externally change the encoding.
Migration notes: Any diagram saved with a version 2.1-2.5 that contains non-ASCII characters can be made compatible simply by changing the encoding with the help of a text editor. The easiest way to do this is to create a new file set with UTF-8 encoding, and paste the content of the old file in.

467495862.Title: Make DiagramView stateless instead of a Wrapper
467495862.Author: prmr
467495862.Body: Change the design so that an instance of `DiagramView` (renamed `DiagramViewer`) becomes a stateless Strategy for drawing diagrams. This will make the architecture simpler because the `Diagram` instance will be more prominently the owner of the diagram state. Also, it will facilitate later decoupling of `DiagramElement` from their views and the complete separation of the diagram logic from JavaFX.
467495862.PlainText:
Make DiagramView stateless instead of a Wrapper

Change the design so that an instance of DiagramView (renamed DiagramViewer) becomes a stateless Strategy for drawing diagrams. This will make the architecture simpler because the Diagram instance will be more prominently the owner of the diagram state. Also, it will facilitate later decoupling of DiagramElement from their views and the complete separation of the diagram logic from JavaFX.

468213047.Title: Implement edge viewing through stateless strategies
468213047.Author: prmr
468213047.Body:
Edge view calculations is currently done using a wrapper `EdgeView` object that can be obtained from the `edge` instance being wrapped. Redesign this code so that the edge viewing is done using a registry of stateless edge viewer strategies. The anticipated design benefits are:

* It will be possible to reuse viewer objects instead of having replicates with identical behavior for each each of the same type;
* It will facilitate achieving a complete separation between the `Edge` clas hierarchy and the viewing code, so as to completely sever the dependency to JavaFX classes from the `Edge hierarchy`.
* It will simplify the code, by avoiding complex state management for edges which currently require a reference to their diagram. By passing the diagram to the strategy, it will be possible to get rid of this dependency.
468213047.PlainText:
Implement edge viewing through stateless strategies

Edge view calculations is currently done using a wrapper EdgeView object that can be obtained from the edge instance being wrapped. Redesign this code so that the edge viewing is done using a registry of stateless edge viewer strategies. The anticipated design benefits are:
It will be possible to reuse viewer objects instead of having replicates with identical behavior for each each of the same type;
It will facilitate achieving a complete separation between the Edge clas hierarchy and the viewing code, so as to completely sever the dependency to JavaFX classes from the Edge hierarchy.
It will simplify the code, by avoiding complex state management for edges which currently require a reference to their diagram. By passing the diagram to the strategy, it will be possible to get rid of this dependency.

509102775.Title: Ignored NullPointerException when no tool selected
509102775.Author: prmr
509102775.Body:
**Description**
When a diagram tab is first opened, no tool is selected. The event handling code incorrectly assumes there is one, so when the user clicks on the canvas or attempts to copy the diagram to the clipboard, an uncaught `NullPointerException` is raised. Because it happens in the event loop, there is no other observable behavior, so this problem can only detected when JetUML is associated with a console.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Open a diagram tab by creating a new diagram or opening a saved diagram.
2. Click on the canvas.

The following exception is raised:

```
Exception in thread "JavaFX Application Thread" java.lang.NullPointerException
	at ca.mcgill.cs.jetuml.gui.DiagramTabToolBar.getCreationPrototype(DiagramTabToolBar.java:177)
	at ca.mcgill.cs.jetuml.gui.DiagramCanvasController.getTool(DiagramCanvasController.java:378)
	at ca.mcgill.cs.jetuml.gui.DiagramCanvasController.handleSingleClick(DiagramCanvasController.java:323)
```

If for step 2 instead of clicking on the canvas, the "To Clipboard" button is used, the exception is:

```
Exception in thread "JavaFX Application Thread" java.lang.NullPointerException
	at ca.mcgill.cs.jetuml.gui.DiagramTabToolBar.lambda$0(DiagramTabToolBar.java:121)
	at com.sun.javafx.event.CompositeEventHandler.dispatchBubblingEvent(CompositeEventHandler.java:86)
```

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release:  2.4 + 90aeca1
 - OS + version: Windows 10
 - Java version:  1.8.0u221
 
**Additional information**
The simplest solution is to enable the Selection Tool by default.
509102775.PlainText:
Ignored NullPointerException when no tool selected

Description
When a diagram tab is first opened, no tool is selected. The event handling code incorrectly assumes there is one, so when the user clicks on the canvas or attempts to copy the diagram to the clipboard, an uncaught NullPointerException is raised. Because it happens in the event loop, there is no other observable behavior, so this problem can only detected when JetUML is associated with a console.
Steps to reproduce
Open a diagram tab by creating a new diagram or opening a saved diagram.
Click on the canvas.
The following exception is raised:
If for step 2 instead of clicking on the canvas, the "To Clipboard" button is used, the exception is:
Configuration
JetUML Release:  2.4 + 90aeca1
OS + version: Windows 10

535835654.Title: Split ClassNode from Interface Node
535835654.Author: prmr
535835654.Body:
In the current design `ClassNode` extends `InterfaceNode`, which is a design weakness because there is no "is-a" relationship between them. They should be sibling subtypes of a common abstract `TypeNode`.

As a result of this patch the design of the nodes has been improved: they have been made slightly more compact when there are methods and interfaces.
535835654.PlainText:
Split ClassNode from Interface Node

In the current design ClassNode extends InterfaceNode, which is a design weakness because there is no "is-a" relationship between them. They should be sibling subtypes of a common abstract TypeNode.
As a result of this patch the design of the nodes has been improved: they have been made slightly more compact when there are methods and interfaces.

545185874.Title: Edge bounds don't include the label in Sequence Diagram
545185874.Author: prmr
545185874.Body:
**Description**
The edge bounds in sequence diagrams don't include the bounds for the label. As a result, when an edge's label extend past the actual edge, the window does not scroll when the label hits the right sides of the view pane, and the label is clipped when the diagram is exported.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Create a new sequence diagram 
2. Create a self-edge and give it a long name
3. copy to clipboard, past in an external application
4. See edge clipped

**Screenshots**
![test](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6033492/71752129-efdec380-2e4b-11ea-9829-f7ec3a007d24.png)

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 2.4
 - OS + version: Windows 10 (applies to all)
 - Java version: 8 (applies to all)
 
**Additional information**
The fix is to add the label's bound in `CallEdgeViewer.getBounds`.

550453868.Title: Package Nodes with nesting depth of 2 or more are not visible
550453868.Author: prmr
550453868.Body:
**Description**
In a class diagram, package nodes can contain other package nodes. However, if a package node has a nesting depth of two or more (package within a package within a package), it is not visible (yet it stay selectable).

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Create new class diagram 
2. Create a new package node, then add a package node within this package node.
3. Add a package node to the inner package node.
4. Only the selection handles are going to be visible

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 2.5
 - OS + version: Windows 10
 - Java version: 1.8.0_231

550457351.Title: Make mode of package node mutually exclusive
550457351.Author: prmr
550457351.Body:
Currently, package nodes can *both* have children *and* contain text, which is not well supported by the rendering because typically the drawing of the children nodes will hide the text. This eventuality should be prevented.

## Solution Alternatives

* Create two variant of PackageNode ("containers") and ("descriptors"), and have them as separate options in the toolbar. Would break backward compatibility. **Solution selected**.

* Use input validation to prevent the setting of text in a node currently containing children, or vice-versa. This is by far the least invasive solution, but it would be able to circumvent it by modifying the persisted version of the diagram (acceptable).
550457351.PlainText:
Make mode of package node mutually exclusive

Currently, package nodes can both have children and contain text, which is not well supported by the rendering because typically the drawing of the children nodes will hide the text. This eventuality should be prevented.
Solution Alternatives
Create two variant of PackageNode ("containers") and ("descriptors"), and have them as separate options in the toolbar. Would break backward compatibility. Solution selected.
Use input validation to prevent the setting of text in a node currently containing children, or vice-versa. This is by far the least invasive solution, but it would be able to circumvent it by modifying the persisted version of the diagram (acceptable).

550996890.Title: Add shortcut keys for tools
550996890.Author: prmr
550996890.Body:
Tools in the toolbar should be accessible using shortcut keys.

The shortcuts are activated by typing the characters `1-9,0,A-Z` to select the tool, where 1 selects the first tool in the tool bar, etc. (0 represents 10 because it is located to the right of the 9 on most keyboards).

Thanks for reporting. It seems that [MacOS Catalina is not a certified system config](https://www.oracle.com/technetwork/java/javase/certconfig-2095354.html).

See also: [Javafx crashes on OSX on JRE 1.8](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/34711128)

There are a lot of incompatibilities with Mac, I'm working on some documentation to clarify this.

551562507.Title: Application crash caused by JavaFX bug
551562507.Author: prmr
551562507.Body:
## Problem
Some MacOS users have reported both predictable and unpredictable crashes when using the application, and in particular in relation to the management of windows and frames. These includes:
* When opening a second diagram (#300)
* When switching ("tabbing") between the JetUML application and other system applications (#322)

## Likely Cause
As far as I could determine this is due to incompatibilities and/or unstable behavior in the underlying JavaFX binaries. The most likely cause is [this JDK bug](https://bugs.openjdk.java.net/browse/JDK-8211304). Similar documented cases include:
* Not using an [Oracle certified system configuration](https://www.oracle.com/technetwork/java/javase/certconfig-2095354.html), see [Stack Overflow: Javafx crashes on OSX on JRE 1.8](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/34711128)
* Running the OS on VMWare [JDK Bug Report: Fatal error when running JavaFX application on OSX running on Vmware](https://bugs.openjdk.java.net/browse/JDK-8146952)

See [this Stack Overflow answer](https://stackoverflow.com/a/52586569) for more details.

## Workaround
This bug has been fixed in Java 12 and is being backported to earlier versions. Please try updating your system to see if it fixes the problem.

## Related Issues:
* #280 Potential System Incompatibilities on MacOS
* #300 Opening second diagram crashes on Mac
* #322 Application crashing on window de-focus
* #385 Unable to open diagram with large Canvas

553806677.Title: Convert Diagram Type hierarchy to enum
553806677.Author: prmr
553806677.Body: Class `Diagram` is currently extended by classes that represent diagram types (e.g., `ClassDiagram`, `SequenceDiagram`. At this point the only purpose of these classes is to hold class-scope information about the type of diagram. This type of information would be better suited to a statically scoped structure. We'll put this into the enum type `DiagramType`.
553806677.PlainText:
Convert Diagram Type hierarchy to enum

Class Diagram is currently extended by classes that represent diagram types (e.g., ClassDiagram, SequenceDiagram. At this point the only purpose of these classes is to hold class-scope information about the type of diagram. This type of information would be better suited to a statically scoped structure. We'll put this into the enum type DiagramType.

555123662.Title: Moving parent node through child goes beyond Canvas
555123662.Author: prmr
555123662.Body:
**Description**
When moving selected nodes, the bounds checks are done on the selected nodes. However, in some cases, such as when a node is in a package node in a class diagram, moving the child node results in the parent node being repositioned. In such cases, it is possible to bring the bounds of the outer node beyond the canvas.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Create a new class diagram
2. Create a package node
3. Create a class node that is a child of the package node (by clicking inside the package node)
4. Select the class node and move it completely to the left (or top, right, bottom) of the canvas.

The parent node goes off the canvas.

**Screenshots**
![jetuml](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6033492/73125962-f4922580-3f7a-11ea-8168-dd4f609a9a15.png)

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 2.5
 - OS + version: All versions
 - Java version: All versions 
 
**Additional information**
 Note that package nodes can nest more than one level deep.

Moving parent node through child goes beyond Canvas

557156436.Title: Extra diagram type suffix get added to file name
557156436.Author: prmr
557156436.Body:
**Description**
When saving a file name, the diagram type suffix (e.g., `.class`) gets added to the name of the file even if it already exists in the name, resulting in a double of the type in the suffix, e.g., `.class.class.jet`.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Create new class diagram 
2. Save As | "Test" -> the file gets saved as `test.class.jet` (ok)
3. Save As again. The name `test.class` appears in the file chooser. Select save -> The previous file is overwritten (ok)
4. Save As again. Remove the last character (`s`), then type it back again, so the name in the file choose is still `test.class`, as in step 3. Select Save -> The file gets saved as `test.class.class` (not ok)

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 2.5
 - OS + version: Applies to all
 - Java version: Applies to all

**Additional Details**
The problem is actually caused by JavaFX's `FileChooser`, which does not by default recognize 2-level file extensions. It seems impossible to solve at the moment.

557157677.Title: Tip of the day feature
557157677.Author: prmr
557157677.Body:
Document the key features of JetUML as documentation modules (a.k.a. "tips"), that can be viewed one by one when the tool starts, or as a whole from the help menu. A user preference should determine whether tips are shown upon startup. 

Each tip should correspond to one cohesive piece of documentation about the tool. Tips should be stored as resources, and comprise at least one block of mark-up text and optionally one image. It should be possible to generate an indexed documentation page by using the documentation.

The documentation modules should have a sequence index. The JetUML properties should remember the last module seen by the user.
557157677.PlainText:
Tip of the day feature

Document the key features of JetUML as documentation modules (a.k.a. "tips"), that can be viewed one by one when the tool starts, or as a whole from the help menu. A user preference should determine whether tips are shown upon startup.
Each tip should correspond to one cohesive piece of documentation about the tool. Tips should be stored as resources, and comprise at least one block of mark-up text and optionally one image. It should be possible to generate an indexed documentation page by using the documentation.
The documentation modules should have a sequence index. The JetUML properties should remember the last module seen by the user.

562244530.Title: Refactor Node Hierarchy Support
562244530.Author: prmr
562244530.Body: Currently the use of the `ChildNode` interface leads to the requirement for many downcasts, when many of the operations actually makes sense as no-op on non-Child nodes. Many of the `ChildNode` implementation use nullable values to represent the absence of a parent, which could be improved through API redesign.
562244530.PlainText:
Refactor Node Hierarchy Support

Currently the use of the ChildNode interface leads to the requirement for many downcasts, when many of the operations actually makes sense as no-op on non-Child nodes. Many of the ChildNode implementation use nullable values to represent the absence of a parent, which could be improved through API redesign.

565922242.Title: Child nodes not staggered when copy-pasted
565922242.Author: prmr
565922242.Body:
**Description**
When nodes are copied, they appear immediately below and to the right of the original node. This required behavior does not occur for nodes that are the child of another node (for example, class nodes nested within a package node).

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Create new class diagram
2. Create a new package node
3. Create a new class node within the package node
4. Select the package node, copy and paste it.
5. The copy appears staggered, which is desired.
6. Select the class node in either package node, copy it, paste it
7. The copy appears immediately on top of the original node, so it's not possible to see it unless it's moved.

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 2.5+ (some later commit)
 - OS + version: All versions
 - Java version: All versions

Child nodes not staggered when copy-pasted

When nodes are copied, they appear immediately below and to the right of the original node. This required behavior does not occur for nodes that are the child of another node (for example, class nodes nested within a package node).

569049715.Title: Moving package nodes using child nodes behaves incorrectly
569049715.Author: prmr
569049715.Body:
**Description**
When moving a package node by moving the underlying child node, some of the behavior is not intuitive:
1. If the child node is deleted, the package node repositions itself to its original position. It should stay at its current position. 
2. Normally moving a child node within a package node should result in moving the node in the left and up directions, and enlarging the package node in the right and bottom directions. However, currently the node is moved in the right and bottom directions up until some point, at which point its starts becoming enlarged. This is probably based on the node's original position.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Create new class diagram, with a package node and a child class node
2. Move the class node to the left
3. Delete the class node
4. The package node jumps to its original position

1. Create new class diagram, with a package node and a child class node
2. Move the class node to the left
3. Move the class node to the right. The original package node follows
4. Keep moving the node towards the right.
5. The package node eventually starts to enlarge.

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 2.5
 - OS + version: All versions
 - Java version: All versions
569049715.PlainText:
Moving package nodes using child nodes behaves incorrectly

Description
When moving a package node by moving the underlying child node, some of the behavior is not intuitive:
If the child node is deleted, the package node repositions itself to its original position. It should stay at its current position.
Normally moving a child node within a package node should result in moving the node in the left and up directions, and enlarging the package node in the right and bottom directions. However, currently the node is moved in the right and bottom directions up until some point, at which point its starts becoming enlarged. This is probably based on the node's original position.
Steps to reproduce
Create new class diagram, with a package node and a child class node
Move the class node to the left
Delete the class node
The package node jumps to its original position
Create new class diagram, with a package node and a child class node
Move the class node to the left
Move the class node to the right. The original package node follows
Keep moving the node towards the right.
The package node eventually starts to enlarge.
Configuration
JetUML Release: 2.5
OS + version: All versions
Java version: All versions

Comments.Body:
As a solution, we will be changing the behavior of how the `PackageNode` gets drawn. The box for the package node will not clip to the bounding box of all its children, in all cases. It will thus no longer be possible to create drawings such as:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6033492/77580777-f7de5a80-6eb2-11ea-8729-bc25c894b6fd.png)

Instead, this diagram would look like:

![test](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6033492/77580903-23f9db80-6eb3-11ea-8dd3-8b5a2d754a77.png)
Comments.Author: prmr

Events.Author: prmr
Events.RenamedFrom: Moving package nodes using child nodes behaves unpredictably
Events.RenamedTo: Moving package nodes using child nodes behaves incorrectly

623391767.Title: Sequence Diagram deletion does not preserve control-flow constraints
623391767.Author: prmr
623391767.Body: **Description** Sequence diagram should only allow the construction of sequence that represent a valid control flow rooted at a single entry point. This constraint is enforced by the `SequenceDiagramBuilder` for adding to a diagram, but not when deleting nodes and edges. Deleting nodes and edges can currently result in an invalid diagram. Additional auto-deletions need to be added to ensure the downstream calls are also deleted. **Steps to reproduce** 1. Create new Sequence diagram 2. Create a diagram like this: 3. Delete the call edge from N1 to N2 **Configuration** - JetUML Release: 2.6 - OS + version: All - Java version: All

627725684.Author: prmr

For `InterfaceNode`s, the interface stereotype is stored in the diagram as part of the name, which is redundant. It is sufficient to show it only as part of the viewer.

This refactoring will require a migration of old diagrams to remove the redundant "interface" in the node name.

627773667.Title: Additional Edge Constraints
627773667.Author: prmr
627773667.Body:
It should not be possible to create circular generalization cycles. This constraint will be limited to two-node cycles until we have a more detailed feedback mechanism. This constraint will not be migrated, so loading old diagrams can violate it, but it does not impact the diagram viewing.

The same should apply to aggregation and association edges.

It should also not be possible to have *both* an aggregation and an association edge between two node, since aggregation is a form of association.
627773667.PlainText:
Additional Edge Constraints

It should not be possible to create circular generalization cycles. This constraint will be limited to two-node cycles until we have a more detailed feedback mechanism. This constraint will not be migrated, so loading old diagrams can violate it, but it does not impact the diagram viewing.
The same should apply to aggregation and association edges.
It should also not be possible to have both an aggregation and an association edge between two node, since aggregation is a form of association.

Events.Author: prmr
Events.RenamedTo: Additional Edge Constraints

628456865.Title: Make AssociationEdge implicitly directed
628456865.Author: prmr
628456865.Body:
Association edges should only be either in one direction, or bidirectional. It should not be possible to flip their directionality once created (for this one can create a new edge).

For consistency, it will be necessary to rename the enums of the directionality property: `{None, End, Both}` to `{Unspecified, Unidirectional, Bidirectional}`.

For consistency, it will be necessary to rename the enums of the directionality property: {None, End, Both} to {Unspecified, Unidirectional, Bidirectional}.

630274242.Title: Auto-activate the Selection Tool
630274242.Author: prmr
630274242.Body:
A common usage scenario is to create a number of diagram elements, then to select them. However, this requires first activating the selection tool, and it's easy for the user to forget this, and end up creating a bunch of unwanted elements instead of selecting one. 

To avoid this issue, the following behavior can be implemented:

**If** the user clicks on the canvas **and** the click is within an object's bound, **then** automatically activate the Selection Tool in the toolbar and select the element clicked.

A special case needs to be added to handled the addition of child nodes of `PackageNode`, however, because in this case we do want to add a node.
630274242.PlainText:
Auto-activate the Selection Tool

A common usage scenario is to create a number of diagram elements, then to select them. However, this requires first activating the selection tool, and it's easy for the user to forget this, and end up creating a bunch of unwanted elements instead of selecting one.
To avoid this issue, the following behavior can be implemented:
If the user clicks on the canvas and the click is within an object's bound, then automatically activate the Selection Tool in the toolbar and select the element clicked.
A special case needs to be added to handled the addition of child nodes of PackageNode, however, because in this case we do want to add a node.

631793369.Title: Refactor the handling of the tool prototypes
631793369.Author: prmr
631793369.Body:
Improve on the following two design weaknesses:
1. The nodes and edges, as prototypes of creating new diagram elements, are unnecessarily split up: they could be treated polymorphically as `DiagramElements` in a lot of the code, thus simplifying it.
2. The tooltip for the node and edge prototypes are indexed in the properties files using integers that represent their sequence, which is brittle. Instead, this key should be stored in an object.
631793369.PlainText:
Refactor the handling of the tool prototypes

Improve on the following two design weaknesses:
The nodes and edges, as prototypes of creating new diagram elements, are unnecessarily split up: they could be treated polymorphically as DiagramElements in a lot of the code, thus simplifying it.
The tooltip for the node and edge prototypes are indexed in the properties files using integers that represent their sequence, which is brittle. Instead, this key should be stored in an object.

686326634.Title: Make connections/edges smarter
686326634.Author: johannesrave
686326634.Body:
**Description**
When more than a few edges are on screen, they interfere with each other and make the diagram hard to read graphically. I spend considerable time repositioning the nodes, just to avoid this. When deciding on an overall layout of a diagram, I think the edge obstructions shouldn't be this much of a concern.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Draw a diagram with lots of inheritance or composition from more than one source node.
2. Look at it.

**Screenshots**
![grafik](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/31467653/91308965-53897880-e7b0-11ea-96f8-2894a2355115.png)


**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 3.0
 - OS + version: Windows 10
 - Java version openjdk version "13.0.2"
 
**Additional information**
I am taking my first steps toward learning JavaFX in my CS degree, so maybe I could tackle this. I haven't yet contributed to OSS yet though, so would need an introduction, but I am looking for a project to contribute to.
686326634.PlainText:
Make connections/edges smarter

Description
When more than a few edges are on screen, they interfere with each other and make the diagram hard to read graphically. I spend considerable time repositioning the nodes, just to avoid this. When deciding on an overall layout of a diagram, I think the edge obstructions shouldn't be this much of a concern.
Steps to reproduce
Draw a diagram with lots of inheritance or composition from more than one source node.
Look at it.
Screenshots
Configuration
JetUML Release: 3.0
OS + version: Windows 10
Java version openjdk version "13.0.2"
Additional information
I am taking my first steps toward learning JavaFX in my CS degree, so maybe I could tackle this. I haven't yet contributed to OSS yet though, so would need an introduction, but I am looking for a project to contribute to.

Comments.Body: Related to #230. However, the latter is the refactoring required to enable the edge layout improvement. There is definitely a plan to improve the edge layout, but making this layout editable is out of scope as it takes away from the minimalist vision for the tool.
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: I absolutely see your point about the scope of JetUML, and expected this perspective. It'd be nice to still enable my usecase of having medium-sized diagrams with a certain amount of edges with robust automatic placement, so I'm glad work in this direction is planned.
Comments.Author: johannesrave

Comments.Body:
## Design Sketch:
1. Provide an _edge category_ scheme in terms of their visual appearance. For example, implementation edges, extension edges, composition edges, etc.
2. Establish a _priority_ scheme so that edges in one category get drawn first (so that it's possible to take their layout into account when drawing other edges). For examples, extension edges go first, then implementation edges, etc.
3. Establish a fix scheme for attaching edges to nodes based on their category. For example, extension edges always attach to the center of a node's side, then immediately after come the implementation edges, etc.
4. The edge's attachment to the node should take into account its position relative to the node, so we avoid edge crossings.
5. This will require to move away from the pure-stateless edge drawing algorithm, since edge layouts will need to be remembered between categories. We will this have a system based on passes through the edges. 

## Visual Examples

In this example the position 0 is used by the extends edge and the position (1) on each side is used by implementation edges.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6033492/106152732-ca55e380-614b-11eb-851f-ff147657d4b7.png)

In this example the current layout has the extends edge group slightly to the left. In the new design this edge group would reclaim position 0, and the second priority edge (implements) would be the one shifted to position 1. Additionally, the edge can be drawn to avoid intersecting with the higher-priority edge.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6033492/106153823-f4f46c00-614c-11eb-935b-5876c261692e.png)
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body:
This looks very good.
However, the problems I had also stemmed from edges crossing nodes, as in my original screenshot - maybe there is a way to incorporate their position and extend into the "pathfinding" as well?
Comments.Author: johannesrave

Events.Author: prmr
Events.RenamedTo: Make connections/edges smarter

692327921.Title: Impossible to add Field nodes to object nodes in Object diagrams
692327921.Author: prmr
692327921.Body:
**Description**
Trying to add a field node to an object node results in the object node being selected instead.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Create new Object diagram 
2. Create a new object node
3. Select field node creation tool in the toolbar to activate it
4. Click on the object node to add the field node.

The object node is selected instead.

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 3.0
 - OS + version: All versions:
 - Java version: All versions
 
**Additional information**
This is a regression from #363 . The problems is that in `DiagramCanvasController.isNodeOtherThanPackageAndField` the code checks the destination node, not the node type to be created. Instead of `FieldNode`, it should be `ObjectNode` that is part of the condition.

```
private boolean isNodeOtherThanPackageAndField(DiagramElement pElement)
{
	return pElement instanceof Node && !(pElement instanceof PackageNode)&& !(pElement instanceof FieldNode);
}
```

703534413.Title: Insufficient name padding for some class diagram and use case diagrams elements
703534413.Author: prmr
703534413.Body:
**Description**
In class diagrams, the name of class and interface elements can exceed the bounds of the object. In use case diagrams, the name gets too close to the bounds (without exceeding them. This is not a problem for other nodes, or for other parts of nodes (e.g., methods and attributes in class nodes).

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Create new class diagram 
2. Create a new class node
3. Type characters in the node's name field until the name exceeds the bounding box.

**Screenshots**
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6033492/93469104-033c9c80-f8be-11ea-8eed-f7fe35d9e1cd.png)

**Configuration**

1. Using a recent version (e.g., 3.0.1) save a class diagram with a dependency edge.
2. Open the saved file with a text editor and change the version number to 3.0
3. Reopen the file.

**Additional information**

713172549.Title: Unable to open diagram with large Canvas
713172549.Author: prmr
713172549.Body:
**Description**
Depending on hardware, a large diagram size (set through `View | Set Diagram Size`) makes it look like nothing happens when a user attempts to create a new diagram or open any recent file that has too large a diagram size.

This problem is due to an [underlying JavaFX bug](https://bugs.openjdk.java.net/browse/JDK-8089835). The workaround is to use a smaller diagram size. Alternatively, [this Stack Overflow post](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/23467797/javafx-nullpointerexception-when-rendering-big-image) provides an alternative that uses command-line arguments to the virtual machine.

**Steps to reproduce**
It is only possible to reproduce this bug when JetUML is open on a display that cannot support the diagram size. 
1. Open JetUML and click `View | Set Diagram Size`, select the max size.
2. Create a new Class diagram (for example)

The following exception will be raised by the graphics system:

```
java.lang.NullPointerException
	at javafx.graphics/com.sun.javafx.sg.prism.NGCanvas$RenderBuf.validate(NGCanvas.java:213)
	at javafx.graphics/com.sun.javafx.sg.prism.NGCanvas.initCanvas(NGCanvas.java:641)
	at javafx.graphics/com.sun.javafx.sg.prism.NGCanvas.renderContent(NGCanvas.java:604)
	at javafx.graphics/com.sun.javafx.sg.prism.NGNode.doRender(NGNode.java:2072)
	at javafx.graphics/com.sun.javafx.sg.prism.NGNode.render(NGNode.java:1964)
	at javafx.graphics/com.sun.javafx.sg.prism.NGGroup.renderContent(NGGroup.java:270)
```

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 2.1 and up
 - OS + version: Windows, Mac
 - Java version: 14
713172549.PlainText:
Unable to open diagram with large Canvas

Description
Depending on hardware, a large diagram size (set through View | Set Diagram Size) makes it look like nothing happens when a user attempts to create a new diagram or open any recent file that has too large a diagram size.
This problem is due to an underlying JavaFX bug. The workaround is to use a smaller diagram size. Alternatively, this Stack Overflow post provides an alternative that uses command-line arguments to the virtual machine.
Steps to reproduce

Open JetUML and click View | Set Diagram Size, select the max size.
Create a new Class diagram (for example)
The following exception will be raised by the graphics system:

Java version: 14

I downloaded JetUml 3.0.2 jar.
Running it with Java 11

```
$ java -version
openjdk version "11.0.8" 2020-07-14
OpenJDK Runtime Environment (build 11.0.8+10-post-Debian-1deb10u1)
OpenJDK 64-Bit Server VM (build 11.0.8+10-post-Debian-1deb10u1, mixed mode, sharing)

Missing documentation which Java version is needed to run

Comments.Body: Thanks for pointing out this oversight. You need Java 14.
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body:
It's documented here, but the version hadn't been updated. It is now up to date.
https://github.com/prmr/JetUML/blob/master/docs/install.md#thin-jar
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body:
thank you for your fast answer!
The version is mentioned some lines above, too....
I think you missed the version update some lines above, still.

```
* **Thin Jar:** A small Java archive (jar) file that does _not_ include any of the dependencies. This option is available for experienced users who just want to download a tiny file and run it from the command-line. The thin jar is OS-independent but requires to have Java 11 or later *and* JavaFX 11 or later installed.
```
Comments.Author: datazuul

785317551.Title: Imperfect shape intersection for rounded rectangle and ellipse
785317551.Author: prmr
785317551.Body:
**Description**
The current viewers do not compute a precise intersection between an edge and a shape that is not a rectangle or a circle. This includes rounded rectangles (states in state diagrams) and ellipses (use cases in use case diagrams).

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6033492/104490108-702b1f00-559e-11eb-862f-3b19f98357dc.png)

The reason for this is that up to now a simpler algorithm is used that computes the intersection with the shape's bounding box. For high-quality rendering of diagrams, it would be nice to compute the intersection precisely.

**Additional information**
The solution should respect the existing design, which is illustrated in the code through method `CircularStateNodeViewer#getConnectionPoint`. The intersection algorithms should be implemented in `GeomUtils` and tested, and then used in the node viewers for state and use case nodes, respectively.
785317551.PlainText:
Imperfect shape intersection for rounded rectangle and ellipse

Description
The current viewers do not compute a precise intersection between an edge and a shape that is not a rectangle or a circle. This includes rounded rectangles (states in state diagrams) and ellipses (use cases in use case diagrams).
The reason for this is that up to now a simpler algorithm is used that computes the intersection with the shape's bounding box. For high-quality rendering of diagrams, it would be nice to compute the intersection precisely.
Additional information
The solution should respect the existing design, which is illustrated in the code through method CircularStateNodeViewer#getConnectionPoint. The intersection algorithms should be implemented in GeomUtils and tested, and then used in the node viewers for state and use case nodes, respectively.

785468484.Title: Edge labels for segmented edges shift when switching selection
785468484.Author: yannsartori
785468484.Body:
**Description**
If you have edge labels on segmented edges (aggregation, composition, or association) and there is at least one class with some attributes and at least one without, regardless of which classes you select, alternating between selecting one with some attributes versus one without moves the labels of all the segmented edges present on the diagram.
The correct behaviour should be it remains static.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Create new class diagram
2. Create two classes
3. Connect the two with an aggregation edge, and label the edge components
4. Add an attribute to one of the classes
5. Switch between the two classes. See the labels moving.

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 3.1
 - OS + version: Mac OS 11.1 and Windows 10
 - Java version OpenJDK 15
785468484.PlainText:
Edge labels for segmented edges shift when switching selection

If you have edge labels on segmented edges (aggregation, composition, or association) and there is at least one class with some attributes and at least one without, regardless of which classes you select, alternating between selecting one with some attributes versus one without moves the labels of all the segmented edges present on the diagram.
The correct behaviour should be it remains static.
Steps to reproduce

Create two classes
Connect the two with an aggregation edge, and label the edge components
Add an attribute to one of the classes
Switch between the two classes. See the labels moving.

Java version OpenJDK 15

802241902.Title: Can not move classes into packages
802241902.Author: NumberC
802241902.Body:
**Description**
When I attempt to move an already existing class into a package, the class does not go into the package. Instead, the class just overlaps with the package.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Create a class
2. Create a package
3. Attempt to drag the class into the package

**Screenshots**
![ClassOverlapping](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/47584014/107050960-dd932f80-6799-11eb-9383-8be3db6b211d.PNG)

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 3.1
 - OS + version: Windows 10
 - Java version: 15.0.1

**Possible Solution**
I think this may be resolved by editing the `mouseReleased()` class in the `DiagramCanvasController` class. As of now, when you `DRAG_MOVE`, it only aligns the node to the grid, instead of adding the node as a child when applicable.
802241902.PlainText:
Can not move classes into packages

Description
When I attempt to move an already existing class into a package, the class does not go into the package. Instead, the class just overlaps with the package.
Steps to reproduce
Create a class
Create a package
Attempt to drag the class into the package
Screenshots
Configuration
JetUML Release: 3.1
OS + version: Windows 10
Java version: 15.0.1
Possible Solution
I think this may be resolved by editing the mouseReleased() class in the DiagramCanvasController class. As of now, when you DRAG_MOVE, it only aligns the node to the grid, instead of adding the node as a child when applicable.

Comments.Body:
This feature is achieved through a different gesture (tapping the <kbd>shift</kbd> key). Please see the [user guide](https://www.jetuml.org/docs/user-guide.html) or enable the "Tip of the day" from the help menu.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6033492/107052551-cce3b900-679b-11eb-8f1d-c2344f07ec69.png)
Comments.Author: prmr

806904866.Title: Refactor the StringViewer
806904866.Author: prmr
806904866.Body: The `StringViewer` class used to draw strings on the canvas creates a very high number of `Text` objects at runtime, and returns overly conservative dimensions for the text. These two aspects can be improved.
806904866.PlainText:
Refactor the StringViewer

The StringViewer class used to draw strings on the canvas creates a very high number of Text objects at runtime, and returns overly conservative dimensions for the text. These two aspects can be improved.

813518663.Title: Improve design of edge constraints checking
813518663.Author: prmr
813518663.Body: The edge validation code requires the construction of an entirely new `ConstraintSet` object for every call, which is not efficient. We can refactor the code so that we only need one `ConstraintSet` per diagram type.
813518663.PlainText:
Improve design of edge constraints checking

The edge validation code requires the construction of an entirely new ConstraintSet object for every call, which is not efficient. We can refactor the code so that we only need one ConstraintSet per diagram type.

828194721.Title: Refactor code involving text to use StringViewer
828194721.Author: yannsartori
828194721.Body:
Currently, there are a couple classes that contain operations involving text (`AbstractEdgeViewer`, `StateTransitionEdgeViewer`, etc) which all reimplement functionalities provided by StringViewer. These areas can be rewritten to utilize StringViewer

The result of this refactoring would make adding features involving text much easier, since there is only one point of contact.
828194721.PlainText:
Refactor code involving text to use StringViewer

Currently, there are a couple classes that contain operations involving text (AbstractEdgeViewer, StateTransitionEdgeViewer, etc) which all reimplement functionalities provided by StringViewer. These areas can be rewritten to utilize StringViewer
The result of this refactoring would make adding features involving text much easier, since there is only one point of contact.

831291369.Title: Installation Issues on Debian
831291369.Author: NumberC
831291369.Body:
**Description**
I am trying to install JetUML on my Debian machine, but it always gives me an error:
```
Exception in Application start method

871343296.Title: Refactor node position so that x and y are no longer properties
871343296.Author: prmr
871343296.Body:
In the design of diagram `Node` elements, the node's position is stored as a two node properties, `x` and `y` respectively. This has the advantage of simplifying the encoding and decoding of diagrams, since position values get serialized like all other node attributes. However, this insignificant advantage comes at a great cost:

1. The `x` and `y` properties are "special" because they should not be handled by the property sheet, thus requiring a whole mechanism to handle _invisible_ properties.
2. It means the `Point` class cannot be immutable (as it should), because properties have to be mutable.

We can greatly simplify the design of the code by not storing a node's position using properties.
871343296.PlainText:
Refactor node position so that x and y are no longer properties

In the design of diagram Node elements, the node's position is stored as a two node properties, x and y respectively. This has the advantage of simplifying the encoding and decoding of diagrams, since position values get serialized like all other node attributes. However, this insignificant advantage comes at a great cost:
The x and y properties are "special" because they should not be handled by the property sheet, thus requiring a whole mechanism to handle invisible properties.
It means the Point class cannot be immutable (as it should), because properties have to be mutable.
We can greatly simplify the design of the code by not storing a node's position using properties.

872549979.Title: Use enums to list property names
872549979.Author: prmr
872549979.Body:
The name of a `Property` in the `Properties` mini-framework is specified using a `String`, which is also used to serialize the property. This is brittle, and makes it hard to track property names through the code. We can use an enum for tighter dependency checking.

For some dependency (e.g., `Generalization Type`), the name of the property is not externalized, so the tool actually uses the internal name of the property in the UI. This should be fixed.
872549979.PlainText:
Use enums to list property names

The name of a Property in the Properties mini-framework is specified using a String, which is also used to serialize the property. This is brittle, and makes it hard to track property names through the code. We can use an enum for tighter dependency checking.
For some dependency (e.g., Generalization Type), the name of the property is not externalized, so the tool actually uses the internal name of the property in the UI. This should be fixed.

907440950.Title: Snapping Node Dimensions to Grid, except when Moving
907440950.Author: prmr
907440950.Body:
Currently, the position of the nodes is snapped to the grid, but their dimensions (width and height) are not. 

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6033492/120195487-dbdbf480-c1ec-11eb-91c7-751a4a89aef3.png)

It might be interesting to find a way to snap the dimensions of the nodes to the grid as well. However, this can be tricky, because simply snapping the diagram elements' bounds in the viewer creates an undesirable "jitter" effect when moving the nodes. For a really smooth experience, the nodes' view should not be snapped when moving them. 

One design idea is to snap the bounding box to the grid multiple while moving. This will naturally snap to grid once the position is snapped to grid.
907440950.PlainText:
Snapping Node Dimensions to Grid, except when Moving

Currently, the position of the nodes is snapped to the grid, but their dimensions (width and height) are not.
It might be interesting to find a way to snap the dimensions of the nodes to the grid as well. However, this can be tricky, because simply snapping the diagram elements' bounds in the viewer creates an undesirable "jitter" effect when moving the nodes. For a really smooth experience, the nodes' view should not be snapped when moving them.
One design idea is to snap the bounding box to the grid multiple while moving. This will naturally snap to grid once the position is snapped to grid.

908269659.Title: Inconsistent FontMetrics Calculations
908269659.Author: prmr
908269659.PlainText:
Inconsistent FontMetrics Calculations

Description
The boundaries of nodes can jump around when some of their text properties are edited.
This is caused by the value of FontMetrics#getDimension being inconsistent, namely, it returns different values for the same input string at different points in the execution of the program. This inconsistency seems to be caused by the bounds of the Text node used to compute the text dimensions being erratically recomputed within JavaFX. Debugging shows that the valid flag in LazyBoundsProperty#get is unpredictably true or false, leading to the correct value when it is false, and some slightly lower and incorrect value when it is true. For some reason, hitting "backspace" when editing a node invalidates the cached value. This is all very mysterious.
This appears to be Windows-specific, as the bug is not reproducible on MacOS.
Steps to reproduce
In user mode:
Create new class diagram
Create a new note node
Add and remove characters over multiple lines, using backspace
Notice the node bounds move around slightly.
In development mode:
Add the following line in FontMetrics#getDimension, below the line Bounds bounds = aTextNode.getLayoutBounds();:
System.out.println(String.format("height=%.1f", bounds.getHeight()));
Create new class diagram
Create a new note node
Type "XXXXXX" in the node's text property, notice a first value (16.0 in Windows with default fonts).
Type backspace, notice a different value (17.0 in Windows)
Type more "X" characters, notice the value eventually reverts to the initial one (16).
Configuration
JetUML Release: at least 3.0
OS + version: Windows
Java version:  14.0.1
JavaFX version: 11.0.2
Additional Information
This issue is not mentioned in the relevant Stack Overflow post, and in fact running the basic example below does not reproduce the bug, so it's due to some deeper interaction.

Events.Author: prmr
Events.RenamedTo: Iconsistent FontMetrics Calculations on Windows

933139255.Title: Feature to insert the stereotype delimiters
933139255.Author: prmr
933139255.Body: This feature will make it possible to add the stereotype delimiters `«` and `»` to text fields in the property editor for elements using the shortcut <kbd>Ctrl</kbd>-<kbd>Q</kbd>. In addition, the cursor will be positioned between the delimiters. This will make it very easy to specify stereotypes in the names of elements.
933139255.PlainText:
Feature to insert the stereotype delimiters

This feature will make it possible to add the stereotype delimiters « and » to text fields in the property editor for elements using the shortcut Ctrl-Q. In addition, the cursor will be positioned between the delimiters. This will make it very easy to specify stereotypes in the names of elements.

975737650.Title: Cannot select overlapped implicit parameters in sequence diagram
975737650.Author: prmr
975737650.Body:
**Description**
In a sequence diagram, if an implicit parameter is position above another one (because of constructor call layouts), one of the two cannot be selected, depending on which one is selected.

**Steps to reproduce**

1. Create new sequence diagram as show below 
2. Make sure one implicit parameter node is position above the other
3. Try to select the node that's not selected.
4. It does not select.

**Screenshots**
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6033492/130261208-1454d9bf-f193-4def-9df6-0c2dbd2e20d7.png)

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 3.3
 - OS + version: All
 
**Workaround**

Reposition the nodes to enable the selection, then reposition as desired.

975746687.Title: Refactor the PersistenceTests
975746687.Author: prmr
975746687.Body:
There are a number of problems with the tests in `TestPersistenceService`:

* The tests do two things, test that the persistence works, and verify that the diagrams are drawn as expected. This should be split up.
* The part that verifies the diagram is not that useful. It just tests the bounds of all diagram elements. If something goes wrong, we don't really know what except that the pixels are off. We should test for specific expectations about the absolute and/or relative positions and dimensions of objects.
* The part that verifies the diagram is sensitive to the OS that the test runs on, because different system fonts lead to slightly different dimensions. See #346 and #400. It's a huge pain to have to adapt the tests for all OSes. The same oracle should be valid independently of the OS.
975746687.PlainText:
Refactor the PersistenceTests

There are a number of problems with the tests in TestPersistenceService:
The tests do two things, test that the persistence works, and verify that the diagrams are drawn as expected. This should be split up.
The part that verifies the diagram is not that useful. It just tests the bounds of all diagram elements. If something goes wrong, we don't really know what except that the pixels are off. We should test for specific expectations about the absolute and/or relative positions and dimensions of objects.
The part that verifies the diagram is sensitive to the OS that the test runs on, because different system fonts lead to slightly different dimensions. See #346 and #400. It's a huge pain to have to adapt the tests for all OSes. The same oracle should be valid independently of the OS.
